txt: Aubrey, William, an original fellow of Jesus Coll. 1571. See Gutch, i. 569.
Yeomans, Francis, scholar of Jesus Coll. 1571. See Gutch, i. 569.
Wilford, John, fellow Jesus Coll. 1571. See Gutch, i. 569.]
Wightman, (—), M.A. probably fellow Jesus Coll. in or before 1572-3.

Powell, David, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 3 March, 1572-3; fellow of All Soul's Coll. 1573, M.A. 6 July, 1576; B.D. from Jesus Coll. 19 Feb., 1582-3, D.D. 11 April, 1583, "Welsh antiquary," vicar of Meifod 1579, sinecure rector of Llansantfraid-yn-Mechan 1588; died 1598; father of Gabriel 1596, and of Samuel 1592. See Ath. i. 568; Fasti, i. 224; Thomas, 254; & Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 260.
Prichard, Hugh, B.A. 16 Oct., 1570, fellow Jesus Coll. 1573, M.A. 27 June, 1573, rector of Lillingston Dayrell, Bucks, 1577. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Roberts, Griffith, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 21, B.A. 17 Dec., 1576; perhaps M.A. 26 June, 1592, and rector of Lampeter Velfrey, co. Pembroke, 1607, and father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Charles, Meredithe, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 16; vicar of Merthir, or Marthrey, co. Pembroke, 1584, and of Graunston alias St. Katherine, co. Pembroke, 1584. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Traherne, John (Traheyerne), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 18; one of these names vicar of Newport, alias St. Woolos, alias St. Gunlin, co. Monmouth, 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lloyde, Thomas, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 17, as Floyd; B.A. 27 March, 1576 (as Lloyd), perhaps vicar of Penryn, co. Cardigan, 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Kingsmell, Robert, of Southants, eq. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 14; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1578, B.A. 12 May, 1579, M.A. 4 Feb., 1582-3; brother of Ferdinando 1581, etc.

Kingsmell, Richard, of Southants, eq. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1578; brother of Ferdinando 1581, etc. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Winston, Walter, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 36 (30?), B.A. 7 Dec., 1576; rector of Pencombe, co. Hereford, 1570-1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Buckland, Christopher, of London, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 21; B.A. 11 April, 1576, rector of Long Bredy, Dorset, 1578. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
David, Evan (Davyd), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 23; perhaps rector of Bridell, co. Pembroke, 1574, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thorneboroughe, Edward, of Hants. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 12 (or 11); perhaps M.P. Ludgershall 1593. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Ellis, Griffith (Ellys), of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 22; sup. for B.A. 15 April, 1575. See Bloxam, ii. 62?
Robinson, Robert, of Cheshire, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 20; B.A. 15 Feb., 1576-7, M.A. supd. 31 Jan., 1580-1.
Floyde, Thomas, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 17; B.A. 27 March, 1576, as Lloyd, which see.
Chessall, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 22; B.A. 19 Feb., 1574-5.
Williams, Griffith, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 23, B.A. 14 July, 1574, M.A. 7 July, 1580.
Herbert, William (Herbarte or Harbarte), of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 18.
Herbert, Griffithe (Herbarte), of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 20.
Davies, John (Davys), of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 18.
Ellis, John (Ellys), of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 16.
Lewes, Rowland, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 19.
Morgans, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 26.
Vaughan, Edward, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 18.
Lewis, Ellys, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 28.
Percy, Henry (Persey), of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 13.
Nicolls, Thomas, of Middlesex, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept. 1575, aged 15.
Basset, Edward, of co. Glamorgan. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 15.
Edmonds, William (Edmunds), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 18.
Hanmer, Lewis, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1575, aged 18.

Pope, Christopher (Poope), of Dorset, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 15; fellow Exeter Coll. 1579-86, B.A. 7 Feb., 1582-3, M.A. 7 July, 1584, rector of Long Bredy, Dorset, 1585. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Boase, 48.
Lewes, Edward (Lewse), of co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Robertes, Ellis, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Edwards, Francis, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20.

Howell, Thomas, born in co. Cardigan, "verbi minister." Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, sup. for B.A. 21 Feb., 1581-2. M.A. 6 July, 1584; perhaps vicar of Llangammarch. co. Brecon, 1579, and of Abernant, with Convil in Elvet, co. Carmarthen, 1589; died 1632; father of James 1610. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Beechfield, Richard (Bechefield), of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 18; B.A. 3 Feb., 1580-1, M.A. 2 July, 1585.
Owens, Humphrey, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 21; B.A. 31 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 5 July, 1583.
Mo(o)re, John, of co. Denbigh, "Verbi minister." Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 36.
Robertes, Ellis, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 18.
Stedman, Henry, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 18.
Williams, John, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 22.
Evans, Hugh, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 20.
Hollins, John, of co. Staff., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 24.
Price, Evan, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 21 March, 1578-9, aged 12.
Sa(u)nders, John, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1578.
Morries, Evan, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1578.
Bartlet, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 1578.
Bedford, Anthony, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 1578.

Pearse, Morice (Peres), of co. Carnarvon, "Verbi minister." Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 9 Dec., 1579, aged 35; perhaps B.A. March, 1579-80, M.A. 7 July, 1581 (as Maurice Pearce), rector of Hinton Martel, Dorset, 1587-8; admon. at Oxford 8 Nov., 1589, as Peirce. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Evans, John, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 9 Dec., 1579, aged 22; one John Jevans vicar of Llanvihangel Cwmdu, co. Brecon, 1588. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyd, Griffin, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 9 Dec., 1579, aged 19; B.A. 7 July, 1580, M.A. 1 July, 1583.
Gibbons, Morgan (Geebons), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 9 Dec., 1579, aged 18.
Lougher, Richard (Lugher), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 9 Dec., 1579, aged 18.
Powel, Philip, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 9 Dec., 1579, aged 22.
Stedman, Nicholas, of co. Stafford, cler. Jesus Coll., (matric.) entry under date 27 June, 1580, aged 25; perhaps rector of Kingsley, co. Stafford, 1579. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wraye, James, of Yorks, "Verbi minister." Jesus Coll., (matric.) entry under date 27 June, 1580, aged 24, B.A. 5 July, 1583, M.A. 23 June, 1586 (as Weare).
Weare, James, of Yorks, Verbi minister. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 June, 1580, aged 24 (perhaps as Wraye), B.A. 5 July, 1583, M.A. 23 June, 1586 (as Weare).
Evans, David, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry under date 27 June, 1580, aged 20; B.A. 9 May, 1586.
Vaughan, Richard, of co. Carmarthen, mil. fil. Jesus Coll., (matric.) entry under date 27 June, 1580, aged 32.
Cauke, John, of co. Stafford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 27 June, 1580, aged 16.

Owens, Cadwallader, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19; B.A. 25 Oct., 1583; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1585, M.A. 11 June, 1588, B.D. 7 July, 1603, vicar of Llanbrynmair 1608, and sinecure rector 1610, and rector of Llanfecher, both co. Montgomery, 1601, until he died in 1617; father of Richard 1622, and of Walter 1632. See Fasti, i. 455n.; Ath. iv. 85; Thomas, 757; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, William, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 18; student of Christ Church, B.A. 14 June, 1585, M.A. 15 May, 1588, proctor 1595. vicar of his native parish Abergavenny 1589, and of Caerwent, co. Monmouth, 1601, rector of Ewelme, Oxon, 1606-29, canon of Sarum and of St. Paul's 1620; died 1629, a benefactor to Jesus Coll. See Gutch, i. 573; & Al. West. 56.

Powell, Griffith, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20; B.A. 28 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 21 June, 1589 (perhaps B.C.L. supld. 12 July, 1593, D.C.L. sup. 23 July, 1599), principal of his college 1613, until his death 28 June, 1620; buried in St. Michael's, Oxford; will at Oxford proved 15 June, 1621 (as M.A.). See Ath. ii. 283.

Wilmot, William (Wilmort), of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 16; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 8 July, 1584; fellow Oriel Coll. 1585, M.A. 26 April, 1589; one of these names rector of Ruskington 1585-1617, another rector of Monksilver, Somerset, 1622-42; perhaps will at Oxford proved 11 May, 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bigge, Jeffrey (Bygge), of Bucks, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 17; B.A. 7 Feb., 1582-3, M.A. 8 July, 1585; chaplain Magdalen College 1589-93, preacher at Horspath 1589, 1591, 1592, master of St. Thomas's Hospital, Salisbury, 1593, and rector of Patney, Wilts, 1593. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Bloxam, ii. 129.

Gwillim, John (Guillims), of co Monmouth, minister, Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 34; licenced 16 May, 1579, to marry Margery Clemente, spinster, of the city of London; rector of St. David's or Llanthewy Skirrid, co. Monmouth, 1577. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Morgans, Richard, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 16, B.A. 16 Dec., 1583 (as Morgan); perhaps M.A. from Oriel Coll. 6 May, 1592 (as Morgans); one of these names rector of Llanfyllin, co. Montgomery, 1602. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Salisburye, John, of co. Denbigh, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 24 Nov., 1581, aged 14; student of Middle Temple 1594 as of Llewenny, co. Denbigh, esq. (s. John); perhaps M.P co. Denbigh 1597-8, 1601, a knight, and died in 1613. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Morgans, Humphrey, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19; B.A. 19 Feb., 1584-5, M.A., 7 July, 1592, then in orders, rector of Newtown 1592, of Cemmes 1614, and of Llanbrynmair (all co. Montgomery) 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Crosley, Alexander, of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 18; fellow Exeter Coll. 1583-97, B.A. 28 June, 1583, M.A. 16 May, 1586, B.D. (sup. 13 Nov.) 1611, rector of Wickham, Hants, 1611. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & Boase, 48.

Parry, William, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from Magdalen Hall 28 Feb., 1583-4, student of Middle Temple 1589, as 2s. David, of Cwmtoyddwr, co. Cardigan (but ? Radnor). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Griffithe, David, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19, B.A. 28 Feb., 1583-4; fellow Oriel Coll. 1584, M.A. 20 June, 1587, rector of Llanllwchaiarn, co. Cardigan, 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stephens, Edward (Stevens), of Bucks, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 16 Dec., 1585, M.A. 10 July, 1589; vicar of Bledlow, Bucks, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Awbrey, Thomas, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 16 (? B.A. from Christ Church 5 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 16 March, 1586-7, B. & D.D. 23 March, 1593-4).
Leeche, Ralph, of Oxon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry dated 27 Nov., 1581, aged 11; perhaps scholar or fellow St. John's Coll. 1583, B.C.L. 1 March, 1590-1.
Musgrove, John, of Devon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 18; B.A. 10 July, 1585 Mr. Case's scholar 18 Oct., 1583, but subscribes 18 Dec., 1581.
Parkens, John, of Dorset, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19; perhaps M.P. Dorchester 1621-2, a merchant. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Petipher, Anthony (Pettiver), of co. Warwick, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 20; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 29 Oct., 1582; father of the next.
Gittins, John (Gyttyns), of London, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 18; P.C. Hartwell, Bucks, 30 or 40 years. See Baker's Northants, ii. 186.
Evans, Gruffyth, of co. Carnarvon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 3 July, 1584, as Griffith Williams.
Vincent, Simon, of co. Derby, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 17; B.A. from New Coll. 17 Oct., 1587, M.A. 15 July, 1591.
Lewes, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 18; perhaps B.C.L. (by accumulation) 14 July, 1603.
Lloyde, Charles, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. Nov., 1581, aged 19; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 17 June, 1585.
Stokes, Richard, of Cheshire, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20, B.A. 15 March, 1582-3, M.A. 8 July, 1586.
Stedmane, William, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 16; B.A. from Christ Church 5 Feb., 1583-4.
Tichborne, Roger (Trutcheborne), of Hants, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 15; "scholaris Mri. Case."
Bishop, James (Bushippe), of co. Warwick, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 14; 'Scholaris Mri. Case.'
Bromwich, Thomas (Bromidge), of Berks, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 19; 'scholaris Mri. Case.'
Lloid, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 17; perhaps M.A. 2 July, 1585.
Peirce, Richard (Pyerce), of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 16; "scholaris Mri. Case."
Standen, William, of London, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 2 Aug., 1581, aged 14, "scholaris Mri. Case."
Boullan, John, of Rouen, France, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 20; 'Scholaris Mri. Case.'
Lewis, Harry, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 March, 1581-2, aged 20; Henry in Mat. Reg.
Williscott, Edmund, of Berks, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 17, "Scholaris Mri. Case."
Bryn, James, of Ireland, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 19; 'Scholaris Mri. Case.'
Vaughan, Cadwallader (Vachan), of co. Carnarvon, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 16.
Ilsley, Thomas, of Berks, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 18; B.A. 30 April, 1583.
Griffith, John (Gruffyth), of co. Carnarvon, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov. 1581, aged 16.
Price, Matthew (Pryce), of Middlesex, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 1 Nov., 1581, aged 13.
Vaughan, John (Vachan), of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 March, 1581-2, aged 18.
Morgans, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 17.
Pughe, William, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Nov., 1581, aged 22.
Billings, Jonas, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1581, aged 26.
Meredith, John, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 25.
Owens, Morris, of co. Montgomery, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19.
Parry, David, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 March, 1581-2, aged 20.
Weston, Robert, of London, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 27 Nov., 1581, aged 14.
Pugh, Edward, of co. Montgomery, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 15.
Thomas, Rice, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1581, aged 20.
Wrighte, Robert, of co. Derby, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 17.
Maddarde, William, of Ireland, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 16.
Stokes, James, of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 27 Nov., 1581, aged 12.
Shepherd, John, of Somerset, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Nov., 1581, aged 17.
Dodde, Thomas, of London, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 17.
Smithe, Thomas, of Oxon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1581, aged 16.

Tynt, John, of Somerset, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 May, 1582, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple 1598, as 2s. Edmund, late of Wraxall, Somerset, gent., deceased; perhaps brother of Edmond 1582. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Aylwin, John, of Sussex, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 March, 1582-3, aged 22; 'scholaris Mri. Case.' See Dallaway's Sussex, i. 200.
Kelley, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 April, 1582, aged 14 (? 24); called "Kellye" in Mat. Reg.
Bettesworth, Peter (Betesworth), of Sussex, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 March, 1582-3, aged 18; 'scholaris Mri. Case.'
Hughes, Lewis, of co. Cardigan, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 21. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Plott, Bartholomew, of Berks, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 22 Feb., 1582-3, aged 13; "scholaris Mri. Case."
Lloyd, Maurice, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 17 (subs. as Moris Lloyd).
Arundell, Thomas, of Cornwall, gent., 'scholaris Mri. Case.' Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1582, aged 13.
Davids, Rees (ap Lewis), of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 19.
David, David ap, of co. Carmarthen, 'cler. fil.' Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 19.
Prowtinge, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 16.
Chadwell, Edward, of Oxon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 April, 1582, aged 10 (? 20).
Jenkins, David, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 18.
Bowen, Thomas, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 19.
Johnes, William, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 24.
Westbury, John, of Wilts, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1582, aged 19.

Pope, Edmund, of London, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 Oct., 1583, aged 16; "scholaris Mri. Case," B.A. from Queen's Coll. 24 Jan., 1585-6, M.A. 4 July, 1588; B.C.L. from All Souls' Coll. 15 July, 1595, D.C.L. 6 July, 1599, an advocate Doctors' Commons 1601, chancellor to the bishop of Rochester, and surrogate to the judge of the prerogative court of Canterbury; died in St. Botolph without Aldersgate Street 1630. See Fasti, i. 282.

Nicholas, John, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 Oct., 1583, aged 17, "Mr. Case's scholar," B.A. 15 Feb., 1586-7; M.A. from New Inn Hall 11 July, 1590, vicar of Eglwys-wrm, co. Pembroke, 1587. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sherwood, Henry, of Somerset, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry dated 18 Oct., 1583, aged 12. "Mr. Case's scholar."
Trevanion, John, of Cornwall, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 18 Oct., 1583, aged 13, "scholaris Mri. Case."
Parrye, Jesper, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 Oct., 1583, aged 15; "Mr. Case's scholar."
Searle, Richard (Searll), of Hants, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 July, 1583, aged 20.
Portrey, John, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 March, 1583-4, aged 19.
Brookes, Thomas, of Northants, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1583, aged 16.

Canon, Thomas, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Feb., 1584-5, aged 17; sometime of Clifford's Inn, a student of Lincoln's Inn 1593, perhaps knighted 30 June, 1623, M.P. Haverfordwest 1625 & 1626, Haslemere 1628-9. See Wood's Colleges and Halls, iii. 573; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Ayshcombe, John (Aishcumbe), of Berks, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 17; scholaris Mri. Case,' s. of Oliver, of Lyford, Berks, esquire, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1600, father of William, 1623. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Gibbon, John (Gybbon), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 July, 1584, aged 15, perhaps B.A. from New Inn Hall 12 Feb., 1587-8; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 13 Dec., 1592, as Gibbons.
Powell, Hugh, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 18; B.A. 15 Feb., 1586-7, student of Inner Temple 1592, as of Tallylyn, co. Brecon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wroughton, Thomas, of Wilts, eq. aur. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 18; (4s. Sir Thomas, of Broad Hinton, Wilts); brother of George 1586, and Giles 1582.
Price, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 18; one of these names rector of Merthyr, co. Carmarthen, 1601. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyde, Griffith, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from New Inn Hall 9 Dec., 1587, B.C.L. 25 June, 1589.
Evans, John, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 16; B.A. 31 Oct., 1590, M.A. 7 July, 1593.
Barnbye, Charles, of co. York, gent., 'scholaris Mri. Case.' Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Feb., 1584-5, aged 14.
Philippes, Richard, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 July, 1584, aged 15.
Cook, John (Coke), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 22.
Evans, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 20.
Morgans, Thomas, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 16.
Davies, John, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 18.
Evans, Thomas, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 18.
Grante, James, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 17.
Jones, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1584, aged 22.

Adams, Nicholas, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 20 ('Scholaris Mri. Case'). Memo.: One Nicholas Adams, of Clifford's Inn, gent., and bar.-at-law of the Middle Temple 1594 (as 2s. Henry, of Paterchurch, co. Pembroke, esquire), M.P. Pembroke 1589, died 22 Oct., 1628, father of William 1623. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Bale, George, of co. Leicester, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Jan., 1585-6, aged 13; a student of Lincoln's Inn, 1591, probably son of Sir John, of Carleton Curlieu, co. Leicester, and father of Sir John. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Nanney, Griffith, of co. Merioneth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 16, "Mr. Case's scholar;" perhaps of Nanney (s. Hugh), M.P. co. Merioneth, 1593. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Lloyd, Robert, of co. Radnor, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 21, Mr. Case's scholar; possibly B.A. from New Inn Hall, 10 July, 1588, and B.C.L. 25 June, 1589.
Awbrey, Walter, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 29 (? 19); B.A. from Christ Church 12 June, 1588, M.A. 15 June, 1591.
Owen, Richard, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1585, aged 19; B.A. 9 May, 1588; perhaps M.A. from New Coll. 4 July, 1594.
Norwodde, Richard, of co. Gloucester, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 21 Jan., 1585-6, aged 12; brother of W. same date.
Norwodde, W, of co. Gloucester, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 21 Jan., 1585-6, aged 11; brother of Rtchard same date.
Wroth, William, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Jan., 1585-6, subscribes "scholaris Mri. Case."
Nicolas, Humphrey, of co. Carmarth., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 19; "Mr. Case's scholar."
Watkins, Thomas, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 20, "Scholaris Mri. Case."
Lloyd, David, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 18; "scholaris Mri. Case."
Lloid, Gruffith, of co. Radnor, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 17; "scholaris Mri. Case."
Willes, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1585, aged 15.

Perrott, (Sir) James, of co. Pembroke, equitis fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 14; student of Middle Temple 1590, as 2s. John, of Carew, co. Pembroke, kt.; Wood says, "a natural son" (his father was lord deputy of Ireland), of Haroldstone, co. Pembroke, knighted in July, 1603, M.P. Haverfordwest 1597-8, 1604-11, 1614, 1621-2, 1628-9, co. Pembroke 1624-5, held office in Ireland; died s.p. 4 Feb., 1636; brother of John 1580. See Ath. ii. 605; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Woodd, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 May, 1586, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from St. Mary Hall 7 Feb., 1589-90, and M.A. 25 June, 1600; rector of Shelsley Beauchamp 1610, and of Astley, (both) co. Worcester, 1612. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Davies, William, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 20 Jan., 1590-1, M.A. 4 July, 1594, perhaps rector of Henry's Moat, co. Pembroke, 1602. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Voyell, George, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 June, 1586, aged 16, B.A. 4 Feb., 1589-90; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 10 April, 1594; admon. at Oxford 29 July, 1597.
Morgan, Nicholas, of co. Monmouth, minister. Jesus Coll., matric. 29 April, 1586, aged 31; one of these names rector of Pontvayne, co. Pembroke, 1580. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Kyffin, Lewis, of co. Denbigh. pleb. Jesus Coll. matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 17; B.A. 28 Nov., 1589; rector of New Radnor, co. Radnor, 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Farr, William, of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18; for one of these names admon. was granted at Oxford 18 Dec., 162c.
Porter, John, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 March, 1586-7, aged 18; B.A. supd. 27 June, 1590.
Hickes, David, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 19; B.A. 16 June, 1589.
Stephenson, Henry (Stevenson), of Berks, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Oct., 1586, aged 25.
Butler, Richard, of co. Worcester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 May, 1586, aged 23.
Spenser, Thomas, of Salop, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 May, 1586, aged 16.
Jones, Matthew, of Salop, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 May, 1586, aged 19.
Parrie, Henry (? of Anglesea), gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 July, 1587, aged 20; B.A. 3 July, 1591; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 10 May, 1594.
Spencer, Nicholas, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1587, aged 20.
Wergley, John, of Bucks, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 July, 1587, aged 25.

Powell, John, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1588-9, aged 18; perhaps B.A. 3 April, 1596, and rector of Cantreff, co. Brecon, 1601-26, and father of Thomas 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Phillips, George, of co. Cardigan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1588-9, aged 18.
Waters, Harry, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1588-9, aged 16.
Dunlee, Edward (Donne Lee), of co. Carmerthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1589, aged 15. See Dounlee in Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Morgan, George, of co. Monmouth, arm. Matric. entry dated 10 Oct., 1589, aged 10, "Mr Case's scholar"; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 4 July, 1598.
Jo(h)nes, John, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 July, 1589, aged 20.
Powell, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1589, aged 14.
Watkins, Lewis, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1589, aged 17.
Havard, John, of co. Brecon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1589, aged 13.

Powell, Lewis, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 14; student of Middle Temple 1595 (as 2s. Morgan, of Pembroke, gent.); M.P. Pembroke 1621 (void 18 May) and 1625, Haverfordwest 1624-5. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Eynon, John, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 8 Nov., 1592; rector of St. Bride's, co. Pembroke, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Adams, Thomas, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 17; of Loveston, co. Pembroke (son and heir of John, of the same), died 1626-7, father of George 1622.
Lloyd, William, of co. Brecon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 18; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1594, as of Towey, co. Brecon. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Muc(k)leston, Richard, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1590, aged 20; B.A. from New Coll. 3 June, 1592.
Bowen, Lewis, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 18; B.A. 21 Oct., 1596.

Vaughan, (Sir) William, of co. Carmarthen, arm. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16, B.A. 1 March, 1594-5, M.A. 16 Nov., 1597, B.C.L. sup. 3 Dec., 1600; LL.D. of Vienna, incorporated 23 June, 1605; of Terracoyd, co. Carmarthen (s. Walter, of Golden Grove, co. Carmarthen), chief undertaker for the plantation in Cambriol, the southernmost part in Newfoundland; brother of John same date. See Ath. ii. 444.

Vaughan, (Sir) John, of co. Carmarthen, arm. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; of Golden Grove, co. Carmarthen; M.P. 1601 and 1621-2, knighted 30 July, 1599 (s. Walter); created lord Vaughan 1621, and earl of Carbery in Ireland 1628; his will proved 23 May, 1639; brother of William same date.

Evans, Jenkin, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 20; B.A. 9 Feb., 1593-4, dispensed towards M.A. 9 Nov., 1605, then in orders, rector of St. George super Ely, co. Glamorgan, 1605. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jenkins, Jevan, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; B.A. 24 Nov., 1596, M.A. 16 July, 1600 (as Evan), rector of Trefilan, co. Cardigan, 1601, as Jevan. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lewis, Lewis, of co. Brecon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1594, as 2nd son of Thomas, late of Brecon town, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Sherrey, Caradock, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18, B.A. 3 Nov., 1595; vicar of Cardiff SS. Mary and John Baptist 1601. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Johnes, Morgan (Johnes), of co. Carmarth., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; possibly bar.-at-law Gray's Inn 1603. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Daniell, Richard, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; vicar of Jeffreystown, co. Pembroke, 1600. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Atkins, Edward, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 16 Feb., 1595-6; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 2 May, 1599.
Evans, William, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 7 April, 1593.
David, John, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 1 March, 1594-5.
Miles, Rimbrun, of co. Glamorgan, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1595-6.
Gwyne, Howell, of co. Brecon, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 13; called " Gwin" in Mat. Reg.
Jones, Philip, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; B.A. 6 July, 1594.
Vaughan, Rowland (Vachan), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18.
Jones, Philip (Jhones), of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 18.
Vaughan, Thomas, of Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 14.
Williams, Morgan, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 15.
Williams, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1591, aged 18.
Williams, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1591, aged 15.
Elliott, Morgan, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 March, 1591-2, aged 14.
Jones, Owen (John), of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 15.
Powell, Richard, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 15.
Johnes, Walter, of co. Carmarth., gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 14.
Davids, Morgan, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16.
Meredith, Howell, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 20.
Elliott, Owen, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1591, aged 16.
Jones, Howell, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18.
Lloyd, Thomas, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18.

Prichard, Edward, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 19; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 21 Feb., 1595-6; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 4 July, 1600; perhaps B.D. from Jesus Coll. 1 July, 1624, rector of Llantrithyd 1598, and of Marcross, co. Glamorgan, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lloyd, David, of co. Merioneth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 19; perhaps B.A. 12 Dec., 1599; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 23 Oct., 1605, B.D. 11 Feb. (or March), 1612-13 (see Floyd); perhaps rector of Leckford, Hants; father of George 1617. See Ath. iii. 1258.

Prichard, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 18; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 21 Feb., 1595-6; student of Inner Temple 1597, as of Llandaff, co. Glamorgan. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Floyd, David, of co. Merioneth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 19 (as Lloyd); B.A. 12 Dec., 1599; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 23 Oct., 1605, B.D. 11 Feb., 1612-13, as Lloyd, which see.
Lloyd, Roderick, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 20; B.A. supl. 28 March, 1599, M.A. 26 June, 1602.
Maddocks, Robert (Madox), of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric.—Oct., 1593, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 5 March, 1597-8.

Lloyd, Hugh, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 20; B.A. 24 Nov., 1596, M.A. 7 July, 1603 (as Lloyd), B.D. 9 July, 1612 (as Floyd); perhaps vicar of Llanfynydd, co. Carmarthen, 1601, etc.; another of these names archdeacon of Worcester 1623-9; made his will 5 June, 1622, proved 8 Sept., 1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Price, Evan, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 19; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall, 22 June, 1598; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 16 April, 1602, B.D. 31 Jan., 1610-11; licenced to preach 1 Feb., 1610-11, vicar of Newcastle 1608, and of St. Tathans, co. Glamorgan, 1611, canon of Llandaff 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Morgan, Hugh, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 7 Dec., 1597; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 4 July, 1605; perhaps rector of Llanthetty, co. Brecon, 1601. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lewis, Thomas, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16; B.A. 30 June, 1601, perhaps incorporated at Cambridge 1606, rector of Tallachddu 1602, and vicar of Merthyr-Cynog, co. Brecon, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, David, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 20, B.A. supd. 14 Nov., 1597; rector of Llanhileth, co. Monmouth, 1612, and of Llanvihangel-juxta-Usk, co Monmouth, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Fryer, Christopher (Frior), of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 26; one of these names rector of Raggiatt, co. Monmouth, 1590, and prebendary of Llandaff 1601. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Nicholas, Thomas, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 15; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 2 March, 1598-9; perhaps rector of Ullinswick, co. Hereford, 1607. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Johnes, Toby, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 20, B.A. 1 March, 1594-5; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 7 Dec., 1597, but the entry is scored out. See O.H.S. xii. 190.
Owens, John, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1595-6, M.A. 23 June, 1599.
Evan, Hopkin, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 14, subscribes Hopkin ab Jevan.
Thomas, William, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 8 Nov., 1594, aged 14.
Williams, Richard, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16.
Hopkins, Jenkin, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 14.
Johnes, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 18.
Griffith, Owen, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16.
Johnes, Thomas, of co. Cardigan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 20.
Thomas, Roger, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16.

Nanney, Richard, of co. Merioneth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1596, aged 21; B.A. 8 July, 1600, M.A. 1 July, 1603, rector of Netteswell, Essex, 1607, of Llangelynin, co. Carnarvon, 1617, and vicar of Towyn, co. Merioneth, 1623. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Vaughan, John, of co. Hereford, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 14 Jan., 1596-7, aged 14; perhaps student of Inner Temple 1605, as son and heir of Rowland, of Newcourt, co. Hereford, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Meyrike, Rouland, of co. Radnor, militis fil. Jesus Coll., matric. entry dated 14 Jan., 1596-7, aged 12; of Gladestry, co. Radnor (son of Sir Gelly, M.P.), M.P. New Radnor 1614. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Gwyn, David, of co. Radnor, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Jan., 1596-7, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1597, as son and heir of John, of Wigmore, co. Radnor, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Nanney, Edward, of co. Merioneth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1596, aged 18; 2s. Hugh, of Nanney, co. Merioneth.
Bowen, Morgan, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1596, aged 18.
Pughe, Rowland, of co. Montgomery, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1599, as of Mathavarn, co. Montgomery. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rogers, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 20, B.A. 19 June, 1601; rector of Rhosilly, co. Glamorgan, 1607. See Foster's Index Eccl.
David, Griffith, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 19 (Griffin in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 30 June, 1601, as Davyes.
Lloyd, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 17; B.A. 30 June, 1601, and perhaps B.C.L. 14 July, 1603.
Lewes, John, of co. Montgomery, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 20; B.A. 17 Feb., 1598-9.
Bowen, Hugh, of co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 16; B.A. 30 June, 1601.
Pretherch, John (Prytherche), of co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 15.
Birtt, Thomas, of Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 18.

Jones, (Sir) Henry (Johens), of co. Carmarthen, militis fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 April, 1598, aged 16; of Abermarles, co. Carmarthen (s. Sir Thomas), perhaps bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1606; one of these names created a baronet 25 July, 1643; brother of Thomas same date. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Lewys, (Sir) John, of co. Cardigan, arm. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 April, 1598, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1599, as of Abernant-Bychan, co. Cardigan, knighted 29 June, 1604, M.P. co. Cardigan 1604-11; father of James 1617. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Williams, Edward, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 17; perhaps B.A. from St. Mary Hall 17 Feb., 1598-9; possibly a student of Lincoln's Inn 1600, as kin to Sir John Popham, kt. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Johnes, Thomas, of co. Carmarth., militis fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 April, 1598, aged 15; possibly student of Lincoln's Inn 1599; brother of Henry same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Powell, Edward, of co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1608. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Griffith, William (Griffis), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 June, 1598, aged 20; B.A. 18 June, 1599.
Atkins, Robert, of co. Cardigan, 'verbi minister,' fil. Jesus Coll., matric.-entry under date 21 March, 1598-9.

Lloyd, Henry, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 16 Oct., 1602; M.A. from Hart Hall 26 June, 1605 (? as Floyd); perhaps beneficed in Pembrokeshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lewis, John, of co. Brecon. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17; perhaps dispensed towards B.A. 8 June, 1604, see O.H.S. xii. 251, and vicar of Broenllys, co. Brecon, 1604. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lewis, Tobias, of co. Brecon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 15; B.A. 1 July, 1603, student of Middle Temple 1603 as 4th son of Thomas, of Brecon, esq., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hopkins, Edward, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18; one of these names rector of Danbury, Essex, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Kemys, George, of co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1614. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Kinvin, Philip, of co. Hereford, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 18; B.A. 1 July, 1603, M.A. 5 July, 1606.
Bevans, Hugh, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 15. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Edmonds, Thomas, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19; B.A. 1 July, 1603.
Jenkins, Henry, of co. "Mountmorthe" (Monmouth), gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17.
Young, Philip, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 16 (? 18).
Powell, Hugh, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 15.
Toy, Griffith, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 17, B.A. 5 July, 1604; rector of Prendergast, co. Pembroke, 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Williams, David, of co. Carmarthen, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 17, B.A. 5 July, 1604, M.A. 28 April, 1607; vicar of Mothvey, co. Carnarvon.

Fortune, More (Morus), of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15; B.A. 29 Nov., 1604, M.A. 2 July, 1610, B.D. 18 July, 1621 (as "Morus"), rector of Panteague, co. Monmouth, 1612, and vicar of Usk, co. Monmouth, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, William, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15; B.A. 4 June, 1605, M.A. 19 April, 1608, perhaps rector of Sudbrook, co. Monmouth, 1612, and of LlanvihangelIstern-Llewerney, co. Monmouth, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.
James, John, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 15; B.A. 4 July, 1605; student of Inner Temple 1611, as of Gwern-y-combe, co. Monmouth. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Evans, Nicholas, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 17; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 15 Feb., 1603-4; student of Lincoln's Inn 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hibberd, Hugh (Hibbard), of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 20; B.A. 1 July, 1603, M.A. 28 June, 1609, vicar of Llywell, co. Brecon, 1618. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Clente, Edmund, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 28; perhaps vicar of Tenbury, co. Worcester, 1629, father of Thomas 1634. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Penryn, William, of co. Montgomery, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 22 May, 1601, aged 14, student of Gray's Inn 1605, as of Rhysnant, co. Montgomery, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Rumsey, Henry, of co. Brecon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1616, as of Crickhowell, co. Brecon, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Johnes, Edmond, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16, rector of Wilcrick, co. Monmouth, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hopkin(s), Morgan, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 17; B.A. 4 July, 1605, M.A. 6 July, 1608, then in orders.
Rice, Edward, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 22 May, 1601, aged 13; B.A. 5 July, 1604, M.A. 10 July, 1607.
Jenkins, Harry, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15; B.A. from Hart Hall 7 Feb., 1604-5.
William(s), Hugh, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 14, B.A. supd. 16 March, 1604-5.
Johnes, Thomas, of co. Glamorg., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 17, B.A. 4 July, 1605.
Jenkins, Henry, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 15.
Prichard, James, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 19.
Williams, Roger, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 17.
Younge, William, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 18.
Nicholas, David, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 14.
Lewis, James, of co, Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 19.

Rogers, Henry, of co. Hereford, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 18; B.A. 21 Oct., 1605, M.A. 30 May, 1608, then in orders, B.D. 13 Dec., 1616, D.D. 22 Nov., 1637, rector of Moccas 1617, of Stoke Edith 1618, canon of Hereford 1616, vicar of Foye 1636, and vicar of Dorston, all co. Hereford; buried under the parson's seat in Withington church, co. Hereford, 15 June, 1658. See Ath. iii. 31; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rogers, William, of co. Hereford, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 16, B.A. 11 July, 1606, M.A. 2 July, 1610, rector of Bishopstowe, co. Hereford, 1612. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Slugg, Stephen, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Jan., 1602-3, aged 19, B.A. 21 Oct., 1605 (subs. "Sluggue"); vicar of Llantwitmajor, co. Glamorgan, 1629. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Parler, John, of co. Hereford, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 July, 1602, aged 14; called Porler in O.H.S.
Jones, Philip, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 July, 1602, aged 18.
Waters, David, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 16; rector of Llandevailog, co. Brecon, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Atho, Henry, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 15; B.A. 10 Dec., 1606, M.A. 1 Feb., 1610-11, then in orders.
Barnarde, Humphrey, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 July, 1603, aged 26.
Toy, Thomas, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 16.

Penrye, Hugh, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1604, aged 19; B.A. from Christ Church 31 May, 1608; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 15 April, 1611, prebendary of collegiate church of Brecon 1615, vicar of Devynnock 1617, of Llwyell 1618, and rector of Llanvillo (all co. Brecon) 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Vaughan, John, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 16, B.A. 6 Feb., 1607-8, M.A. 9 July, 1612, then in orders; perhaps vicar of Beccles, Suffolk, 1614-38, vicar of Llanstadwell, co. Pembroke, 1620-3, or rector of Garthbeibio, co. Montgomery, Jan.-Dec., 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hughes, William, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 21; B.A. 1 March, 1605-6, rector of Michaelston alias Llanmihangel-juxta-Cowbridge, co. Glamorgan, 1608, vicar of Llancarvan, co. Glamorgan, 1617, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gamage, William (Gamedge), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 20; B.A. 17 Dec., 1607, vicar of Eglwysilian, co. Glamorgan, 1614. See John 1601. See Ath. ii. 350; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Parry, Hugh, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 18; B.A. 20 May, 1607, M.A. 12 June, 1616, rector of Llanarmon DyffrynCeiriog, co. Denbigh, 1619. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Harries, Roland, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 April, 1604, aged 19; B.A. 17 Dec., 1607, M.A. 2 July, 1610, rector of Coytiff, co. Glamorgan, 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Atkins, William (Atkens), of Somerset, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 May, 1604, aged 18; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 12 Nov., 1607.
Miles, Lewis (Myles), of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. entry dated 15 March, 1604-5, aged 11.
Jones, Matthew, of co. Montgom., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 17; B.A. 31 Jan., 1605-6.
Griffith, Jeffrey (Griffis), of co. Montgomery, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1604, aged 14.
Burchill, Fulbert, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 15.
Griffith, Philip, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 19.
Quarrell, John, of co. Radnor, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 19; B.A. 31 Oct., 1608, M.A. 9 July, 1612, vicar of Llanvihangel Crucorney, co. Monmouth, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hughes, William, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 17; B.A. 6 July, 1609, vicar of Llangathen, co. Carmarthen, 1618, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Spicer, William, of co. Carnarvon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 19; student of Lincoln's Inn 1607. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Roberts, Griffith, of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 21; B.A. 28 Jan., 1610-11, as Griffin. See O.H.S. x. 371.
Morgans, David, of co. Carmarthen, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 18; B.A. 20 Feb., 1608-9, as Morgan.
Jones, Richard, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 18; B.A. 8 April, 1608, M.A. 12 June, 1616.
Phillipps, David, of co. Brecon, pleb, Jesus Coll., matric. entry 26 April, 1605, aged 14; subscribes "paup. schol."
Roberts, Abednego, of co. Pembroke, cler fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 17.
Evans, Erasmus, of co. Montgomery, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 18.
Jenkins, William, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 18.
Harris, Henry, of co. Worcester, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 12.
Rice, Hugh, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 17.
Jones, Evan, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1605, aged 19.
Thomas, John, s. Tho., of Coytchurch, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1606-7, aged 22.

Howell, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 18; B.A. 20 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 9 July, 1612, B. and D.D. 5 (or 8) July, 1630 (s. Thomas and born at Bryn, co. Carmarthen, or Llangammarch; co. Brecon), chaplain to Charles I., rector of West Horsley, Surrey, 1625, until sequestered in 1646, rector of St. Stephen's, Walbrook, 1635-41, canon of Windsor 1636, rector of Fulham 1642, bishop of Bristol 1644, until his death 1646; buried in his cathedral; brother of James 1610, father of George 1658. See Ath. iv. 804; Foster's Index Eccl.; Add. MS. 15,670, p. 164; & D.N.B.

Mansell, Francis, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 18; B.A. 20 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 5 July, 1611; fellow All Souls' Coll., B. and D.D. 3 July, 1624, rector of Easington, Oxon, 1630, and of Elmley chapel, Kent, 1631, the famous royalist principal of Jesus Coll. 1620-1, and again 1630 until deprived in 1648, restored 1660, resigned 1661 (s. Sir Francis, bart.), prebendary of St. Davids, treasurer of Llandaff 1631; died 1 May, 1665; half brother of John 1627. See Gutch, i. 585; Burrows, 503; Foster's Index Eccl.; & Add. MS. 15,670, pp. 31, 75, 125.

White, John, of co. Pembroke, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1618, autumn reader or bencher 1641 (as 2s. Henry, of Henllan, co. Pembroke, esq.); licenced 30 Aug., 1631, to marry Mary, daughter of Thomas Style, of Little Missenden, Bucks, gent.; on the parliamentary committee for plundered ministers, M.P. Southwark (L.P.) 1640, until his death 30 Jan., 1644-5; buried in the Temple church 1 Feb.; brother of Griffith 1607. See Ath. iii. 144; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Wogan, John, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 19; perhaps of Wiston (s. William), M.P. co. Pembroke 1614, 1621-2, 1625, 1626, 1628-9, April-May 1640, and 1640 (L.P.) until his death in 1645. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Barlowe, John, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1607, aged 18; a student of Middle Temple 1605 (as son and heir of George, of Slebeech, co. Pembroke, esquire), a royalist colonel. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Rice, Henry, of co. Carmarthen, militis. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1607, aged 21; B.A. 17 Dec., 1607; of Newton, co. Carmarthen, 1s. Sir Walter Rice Fitz Vryan; brother of Thomas 1607, and ancestor of Griffin last-named.

Davies, Roger (Davyes), of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 19; B.A. 17 Nov., 1610, M.A. 30 June, 1614, then in orders, vicar of Clifford, co. Hereford, 1621. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Morgan, William, of co. Monmouth, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1607-8, aged 16; B.A. 5 Dec., 1611, M.A. 30 June, 1614, vicar of Llangwm and Warthacombe, co. Monmouth, 1612. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, Christopher, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 17; B.A. 5 Dec., 1611, M.A. 30 June, 1614; student of Gray's Inn 1616, of Usk, co. Monmouth. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Lorte, John, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1610, as 2s. Roger, of Stacpoole, co. Pembroke, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rice, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, militis. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1607, aged 19, B.A. 17 Dec., 1607; 2s. Sir Walter Rice Fitz Vryan, of Newton; brother of Henry 1607.
Lewis, Richard, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from Hart Hall 6 Feb., 1609-10.
Proger, Charles, of co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1607-8, aged 20; B.A. 22 April, 1611, as Progers.
Stidman, John, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 19. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
White, Griffith, of co. Pembroke, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 19; brother of John 1607.
Lewis, John, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1607, aged 20; B.A. 17 Nov., 1610.
Roblyn, Henry, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1607, aged 26; B.A. 17 Dec., 1607.
Batten, Simon, of Wilts, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 17; B.A. 25 Oct., 1609.
Long, Henry, of Wilts, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 18; died before 1621.
Blethin, 'Rheseus,' of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1607, aged 19.
M(e)yricke, Reese, of co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1607, aged 17.
Gwillim, David, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1607-8, aged 18.
Lucas, Michael, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 15.
Thomas, James, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 19.
Warren, Thomas, of Pembroke, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1607, aged 18.

Owen, Morgan, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Dec., 1608, aged 23; chaplain New Coll., B.A. 5 July, 1613 (as Owen Morgan); M.A. from Hart Hall 4 June, 1616; created D.D. from Jesus Coll. 31 Aug., 1636 (3s. Owen, of Mothvey,. co. Carnarvon), well beneficed in Wales; perhaps rector of Port Eynon, co. Glamorgan, 1619, canon of St. Davids 1623, prebendary of collegiate church of Brecon 1626, precentor 1637, rector of Newtown 1640, bishop of Llandaff 1639, until his death at Mothvey 4 March, 1644-5. See Ath. iv. 803; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Wattes, Richard, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 April, 1609, aged 18.

Howell, James (Howells), of co. Carmarthen, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 16; B.A. 17 Dec., 1613, fellow 1623 (2s. Thomas), secretary to Emanuel Lord Scrope, Earl of Sunderland, president of the North, M.P. Richmond, Yorks, 1628-9, a clerk of the council—King's historiographer—the first appointed; buried in the Temple church 3 Nov., 1666, as Powell; brother of Thomas 1707. See Ath. iii. 744; & D.N.B.

Prichard, Thomas, of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 19 (paup. schol.); B.A. 4 Nov., 1612, M.A. 4 July, 1615, B. and D.D. 9 July, 1628, rector of Letterston 1620, and vicar of Nevern 1625, rector of Langwm 1628, prebendary of Brecon 1629, rector of Stacpoole Elidor 1631, all co. Pembroke, and rector of Llandyssil, co. Cardigan, 1632. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Williams, (Sir) Charles, of co. Monmouth, arm. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 19; student of Inner Temple 1612, as of Llangibby Castle, co. Monmouth, gent.; knighted 10 April, 1621, M.P. co. Monmouth 1621-2 and 1640 (L.P.), until his death in 1641. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Williams, David, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 18, B.A., 27 Jan., 1613-14; perhaps rector of Horsington, Somerset, 1632, and vicar of Ambleston, co. Pembroke, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Thomas, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 17, B.A. 18 Feb., 1614-15; vicar of Llandebie 1619, and of Abergwilly, co. Carmarthen, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Carne, John (s. John), of co. Glamorgan, militis. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 18; of Ewenny, co. Glamorgan, sheriff 1620, buried at Ewenny 27 March, 1644, father of Edward 1638.
Holker, Richard, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 May, 1610, aged 21, " paup. schol.;" B.A. from St. Mary Hall 7 Feb., 1611-12.
Lewis, John, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 19; B.A. from Christ Church 8 July, 1613.
Wilcocks, John, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 18, B.A. 5 Dec., 1611.
Walter, Philip, of co. Carmarthen. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 19, "paup. schol."
Thomas, Powell, of co. Glamorgan, minister fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 22.
Owens, Griffith, of co. Brecon. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 16; "paup. schol."
Phillippes, James, of co. Cardigan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 16.
Williams, Moris, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 19.
Price, Hopkin, of co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 16.
Jones, Roger, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 20.
Evans, John, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 June, 1610, aged 17.
Jones, Gervase, of co. Glamorg., cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 18; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 19 June, 1615; Jarvase of St. Michael's parish, Oxford, will proved 6 Aug., 1621.
Owen, Richard, of co. Monmouth. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 19; "paup. schol." B.A. 30 June, 1614, M.A. 10 July, 1618; incorporated at Cambridge 1624.
Madocks, John, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 17; B.A. 4 July, 1615 (incorporated at Cambridge 1618), M.A. 9 July, 1619.
Hawkins, Nicholas, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 19; B.A. 4 Nov., 1612.
Meyrick, William (Mirick), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 16.
Griffith, John (Gruffins), of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 19.
Per(r)ott, William, of co. Brecon, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1611, aged 19.

Chedell, Rowland, of Isle of Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 17; B.A. 5 Feb., 1618-19, M.A. 6 June, 1621, B. & D. D. 30 June, 1632, incorp. at Cambridge; licenced 15 July, 1633, to marry Margery Chetle, of St. Clement Danes, widow; rector of Llandysnam, co. Anglesey, 1620, and of Llanfachell 1621, dean of Bangor 1625, rector of Llanfihangel-Itraith, co Merioneth, 1625, and treasurer of Bangor Cathedral 1625, rector of Llandegvan, co. Anglesea, 1626, and of Llanvaethlu, co. Anglesea, 1649. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Prichard, Roger, of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 15; B.A. 9 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 29 June, 1622, incorporated at Cambridge 1626, vicar of Kidwelly, co. Carmarthen, 1626, rector of Llangwnllo, co. Cardigan, 1630, and vicar of St. David alias Llanvaes, co. Brecon, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, John, of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 12 May, 1615, aged 14; B.A. 9 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 29 June, 1622; incorporated at Cambridge 1622, rector of Morvil 1620, of Llanstadwell 1623, and of Stainton, co. Pembroke, 1625. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hughes, Lewis, of co. Glamorgan, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 17; B.A. 14 July, 1618; perhaps licenced 16 July, 1625, to marry Anne, widow of John Smith, citizen and draper of London. See Foster's Index Eccl. & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Stephens, William (Stevens), of Oxon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll. 1618-23, B.A. 15 Dec., 1618, M.A. 14 June, 1621, fellow 1623-8; rector of Easington, Oxon, 1624. See Bloxam, v. 82; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Penryn, Humphrey, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 20; B.A. 29 Nov., 1615, M.A. 9 July, 1619, then in orders; rector of Hordley, Salop, 1626, vicar of Welshpool, co. Montgomery, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Morgans, Lewis, of co. Brecknock, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 18; B.A. 9 Dec., 1617, M.A. 19 June, 1620 (as Morgan), vicar of Brecknock St. John 1621, and rector of Llanthetty, co. Brecon, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, William, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 20; B.A. 9 Dec., 1617; M.A. from Christ Church 17 May, 1620; perhaps rector of Goytrey, co. Monmouth, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Evans, Walter, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 14 July, 1618, M.A. 5 July, 1626, vicar of St. Clears 1619, and of Llanboidy, co. Carmarthen, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Williams, Thomas, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 19, B.A. 9 Dec., 1617, M.A. 19 June, 1620; perhaps rector of Newtown, co. Monmouth, 1618. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Davies, John, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 May, 1615, aged 22; B.A. 29 Nov., 1615, perhaps vicar of Llanilar, co. Cardigan, 1620. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Maynard, John, of Devon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. from Exeter Coll., 15 June, 1616, M.A. 26 June, 1619, fellow 1622 (s. John); died 1625. See Boase, 61.
Wade, Richard, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 17, B.A. 9 Feb., 1617-18; vicar of St. Donat's, co. Glamorgan, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Williams, Howell, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 18, B.A. 7 July, 1619; vicar of Llanrian, co. Denbigh, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Vaughan, Edward, of co. Denbigh, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 16, B.A. 8 Dec., 1619, M.A. 29 June, 1622.
Barret, Michael, of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 17; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 1 July, 1618.
Turbervill, John, of co. Brecon, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 17, B.A. 29 Nov., 1615.
Dawkins, Evan, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 9 Dec., 1617.
Morgans, David, of co. Glamorgan, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 18.
Davis, William, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 May, 1615, aged 16.
Williams, Rice, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 17.
Johnes, Richard, of co. "Car.," gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 18.
Rice, David, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 May, 1615, aged 16.
Tayler, Robert, of Somerset, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 19.
Gwyn, Edward, of co. Brecon, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 17.

Seys, David, of co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 21 March, 1616-17, aged 14; B.A. from Christ Church 5 July, 1621; fellow All Souls' Coll., M.A. 9 April, 1624, B.C.L. 30 Oct., 1626; died 1 Jan., 1632, buried in All Souls' chapel, admon. at Oxford 30 May, 1632. See Gutch, i. 296.

Jones, Walter, of co. Monmouth, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 20; B.A. 1 July, 1616, M.A. 9 July, 1619, rector of Llanvayer Kilgedine, co. Monmouth, 1622, and of Christ Church, als. Trinity, co. Monmouth, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lewis, James, of co. Cardigan, equitis fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 March, 1616-7, aged 15; of Abernant, co. Cardigan (probably son of Sir John, 1598), M.P. co. Cardigan, 1624-5, 1625, 1626, 1628-9, and April-May, 1640. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Snelgrave, Thomas, of Kent, arm. fil. nat. max. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 18, B.A. 14 July, 1618 (as "eq. aur. fil."); student of Middle Temple 1620, as son and heir of Henry, of Beckenham, Kent, kt. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Powell, Valentine, of co. Monmouth, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 16; B.A. 9 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 6 July, 1620, rector of Llanvetherine alias Llanveryn, co. Monmouth, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Walweyn, Richard, of co. Hereford, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 18, B.A. 10 July, 1617, M.A. 1 June, 1620; rector of Madresfield, co. Worcester, 1620. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lloyd, David, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 March, 1616-7, aged 24; perhaps B.A. 5 Feb., 1620-1, M.A. 1 July, 1624; one of these names beneficed in co. Cardigan. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Awbrey, Richard, of co. Brecknock, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1616-17, aged 23; rector of Llanwrthwl, co. Brecon, 1623, vicar of Boughrood, co. Brecon, 1630. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thomas, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 3 May, 1616, aged 14, B.A. 8 Dec., 1619; perhaps vicar of Llanddewi (in Gower), co. Glamorgan, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jenkins, Thomas, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 19; B.A. 9 Feb., 1617-18, rector of Lanvihangel Ystern Llewern, co. Monmouth, 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Thomas (Prise), of co. Cardigan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1617, as of Llanbredy, co. Cardigan. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Carne, William, of co. Glamorgan, arm. fil. nat. max. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 17; of Nash, co. Glamorgan, eldest son of Sir Edward, a teller of the Exchequer, etc.
Lewis, William, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18; B.A. 7 July, 1619; perhaps rector of Peterstone, co. Glamorgan, 1639. See Foter's Index Eccl.
Jones, Nathaniel, of co. Glamorg., cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18; B.A. 14 July, 1618, M.A. 11 May, 1621; rector of Merthyr Tydvil, co. Glamorgan, 1621.
Watkins, George, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 March, 1616-17, aged 15, B.A. 1 June, 1620; vicar of Bassaleg, co. Monmouth, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Kemys, Edmund (Kemis), of co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18; (s. Rhys, of Llanvair Castle, co. Monmouth); brother of Henry 1617.
Fane, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 16; perhaps of Fairlane, Kent, died unmarried Sept., 1692, aged 90.
Awbrey, Havard, of co. Brecknock, eq. aur. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 17; 5th son of Sir Edward of Tredomen.
Evans, Evan, of co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 16; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 26 Nov., 1619.
Lewis, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18; B.A. 8 Dec., 1619, M.A. 18 June, 1623.
Walwyn, Peter, of London, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 16, B.A. 21 Feb., 1619-20, M.A. 29 June, 1622.
Evans, Humfrey, of co. Glamorgan (co. Cardigan written over), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1616-7, aged 18.
Owenn, Richard, of co. Montgomery, arm. fil. nat. 3. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 20; B.A. 9 Dec., 1617.
Morgans, Edward, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 14; B.A., July, 1619, as Morgan.
Jeffreys, Hugh, of co. Brecknock, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 15; sup. for B.A. 24 Nov., 1619.
Jones, William, of co. Monmouth, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1616-17, aged 17; B.A. 6 July, 1620.
Kemeys, Henry, of co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1616-17, aged 14; brother of Edmund 1616.
Price, William, of co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1619-20.
Selbie, John, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 20; B.A. 4 Feb., 1618-19.
Rumsey, Edward, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 15; B.A. 8 Dec., 1619.
Davies, Jenkin, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1616-17, aged 19.
Elliatts, Evan, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1616-17, aged 17.
Powell, William, of co. Brecknock, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 19.
Lloyd, Richard, of co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18.
Lloyde, David, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 22.
Owen, Thomas, of co. Montgomery, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18.
Lloyd, John, of co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May 1616, aged 19.
Walter, John, of Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18.
Colton, James (Coulton), of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 23; B.A. 9 Feb., 1617-18, vicar of St. Twinnells, father of the last named.
Price, Walter, of co. Carmarthen, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1618-19, aged 23.
Rimbron, Morgan, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1619, aged 20.
Evans, John, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 Oct., 1620, aged 19; B.A. 8 Feb., 1620-1.

Davis, Francis, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 26 Feb., 1624-5, M.A. 14 March, 1627-8, B.D. 22 June, 1640, created D.D. 21 May, 1661, vicar of Pentyrch, co. Glamorgan, 1630, rector of Llangan, 1638, preb. of St. Andrew alias St. Thomas Baschurch, in Llandaff, 1639, and rector of St. Andrew 1667, rector of Llangattock, co. Brecon, 1667, and of Bedwas, cos. Monmouth and Glamorgan, 1668, preb. of William Jones alias Fairwater, in Llandaff, 1668, archdeacon of Llandaff 1660, bishop 1667, until his death 14 March, 1674-5. See Ath., iv. 849; Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & D.N.B.

Jones, Walter, of Oxon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 16, B.A. 1 July, 1624, M.A. 17 April, 1627; B.D. from Christ Church 26 June, 1640, D.D. 19 Oct., 1660, rector of Hulton, Essex, 1639-42, of Eisey, Wilts, 1643, and of Sunningwell, Berks, 1649-72. canon of Westminster 1660, and of St. Paul's 1660, subdean of the king's chapel and of Westminster Abbey, treasurer of Chichester 1667, until his death 12 July, 1672; buried in the abbey 15th; father of Henry 1668. See Chester's West. Abbey Reg. 177; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Lloyd, John, of co. Denbigh, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 21 June, 1625; perhaps rector of Marchwiell, co. Denbigh, 1648, canon of St. Asaph 1660, warden of Ruthin 1663, and archdeacon of Merioneth 1663, and rector of Llandudno, co. Carnarvon, 1666, sinecure rector of Llansannan (1st portion) 1660, (died 1668); and perhaps brother of Humphrey 1628. See Thomas, 252, 396.

Gibbs, William, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1621-2, aged 16; B.A. 21 June, 1625, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1632 (as son and heir of David, of Neath, co. Glamorgan), licenced 19 April, 1633, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of George Morgan, of All Hallows, Barking, merchant; brother of John 1624. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Price, Charles (Prise), of co. Radnor, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 21; B.A. 19 Nov., 1621, M.A. 21 June, 1625; perhaps vicar of Watlington, Oxon, 1625, or sequestered to the vicarage of Glascombe, Radnor, 1647, by the Westminster assembly. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 214.

Herbert, Charles, of co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 29 Oct., 1623, M.A. 5 July, 1626, vicar of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, 1632, rector of Ystradgunlais, co. Brecon, 1634, and of Llanvillo, co. Brecon, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Woodds, Jacob, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1621, aged 18, B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 21 June, 1625; perhaps vicar of Llandevalley, co. Brecon, 1634, and prebendary in collegiate church of Brecon 1638, as James Wood. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hardwicke, Humphrey, of co. Worcester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 19; rector of Witton in Droitwich, co. Worcester, 1629; perhaps sequestered to rectory of Much Hadham, Herts, 1645. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Add. MS. 15,669.

Lewes, Lewis, of co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 16; B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 7 July, 1625, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1633, as son and heir of William, of Carmarthen town, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Jeffreyes, Richard, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1621, aged 20; B.A. 19 Nov., 1621, M.A. 7 July, 1625; vicar of Llanrwst, co. Denbigh, 1638, and rector of Llanllechid, co. Carnarvon, 1642. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Parry, William, of co. Denbigh, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 16; B.A. 19 Feb., 1624-5; possibly bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1637, as son of Simon of Nantlwyd, co. Denbigh, esq., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Wynne, John, of Flints, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. entry 2 Nov., 1621, aged 14, B.A. 21 June, 1625; perhaps bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1635, as son and heir of John, of Mold, Flints, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Gamage, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 17; B.A. 29 Oct., 1623, student of Gray's Inn 1624, as son of Edward, of Newcastle, co. Glamorgan, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Prichard, Walter, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 16; B.A. 6 Feb., 1623-4, M.A. 5 July, 1626, vicar of Trelech or Bettwys, co. Carmarthen, 1628. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Holland, Timothy, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Nov. 1621, aged 19; B.A. 23 June, 1623, M.A. 17 April, 1627, rector of Compton Greenfield, co. Gloucester, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Madrin, Hugh, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 26 Feb., 1621-2, M.A. 1 July, 1624, rector of Newtown, co. Montgomery, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Samuel, of co. Radnor, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 21; B.A. 9 Nov., 1622, M.A. 7 July, 1625, vicar of Aberavon, co. Glamorgan, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Matthew, Morgan, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1621, aged 21; B.A. 25 June, 1621, rector of Bittadon, Devon, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bolde, Henry (Bould), of co. Carnarvon, 'cler. fil.' Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1621, aged 19; B.A. 19 Nov., 1621, M.A. 1 July, 1624.
Breese, David, of London, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 23 June, 1623, M.A. 17 April, 1627 (? as Bryze).
Williams, Christopher, of co. Monmouth, arm. fil. nat. (3). Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1621-2, aged 19, B.A. 26 Feb., 1621-2.
Bowen, David, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 9 Nov., 1622, M.A. 21 June, 1625.
Adams, George, of co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 Jan., 1621-2, aged 18; son of Thomas 1590, sold Loveston.
Jones, Richard, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 22; B.A. 26 Feb., 1621-2.
Nicholas, John, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1621-2, aged 16; B.A. 13 July, 1625.
Powell, John, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1621-2, aged 20, B.A. 26 Feb., 1621-2.
Edwards, Edward, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 20; B.A. 26 Jan., 1623-4.
Thomas, Lewis, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 Jan., 1621-2, aged 20, B.A. 9 July, 1624.
Thomas, Lewis, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 17, B.A. 29 Oct., 1623.
Price, Walter, of "Rodneiensis" (perhaps Radnor), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 21.
Andrewes, Andrew, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 17.
Davies, Richard, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 20.
Bayly, William, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 21.
Browne, Walter, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 17.
Glynn, Matthew, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 19.
Shepheard, Thomas, of Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18.

Owen, Evan, 3s. George, of Henllys, co. Pembroke, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1622, aged 23; B.A. same day, M.A. 21 June, 1625, created B.D. 31 Aug., 1636, and D.D. 12 April, 1643, rector of Newport 1622, of Llanychllwyddog 1626, and of Walwyns Castle (all co. Pembroke) 1638, chancellor of St. Davids 1644, until his death 30 Dec., 1662; brother of George 1643. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Owen, Francis, s. Lewis, rector of Herbrandston, co. Pembroke, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 31 Jan., 1622-3, aged 27; B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 21 June, 1625, vicar of Monckton St. Nicholas 1621, and rector of Hodgeston, co. Pembroke, 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pettingall, Batholomew, s. Barthol., of Pontypool, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 June, 1622, aged 17; B.A. 9 Nov., 1622, M.A. 21 June, 1625; vicar of Dilwyn, co. Hereford, 1628, and canon of Hereford 1628. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Johnes, John, of co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1622, aged 20; B.A. 9 Nov., 1622, M.A. 21 June, 1625; one of these names rector of Llanfoist, co. Monmouth, 1636, and of Llanvapley 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.
War(w)icke, John, of Radnor, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 May, 1622, aged 20; rector of Llangeitho, co. Cardigan, 1630. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Griffith, Walter, s. Giles, of Monmouth, co. "Mon.," pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 June, 1622, aged 18; B.A. 21 June, 1625, M.A. 20 May, 1628.
Vanne, Robert, s. Thomas, of Caerwent, co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 31 Jan., 1622-3, aged 21, B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3.
Barrett, Thomas, s. Richard, of Carmarthen (town), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1622-3, aged 20; B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3.

Griffith, Rice, s. Henry, of St. Patrick, co. Pembroke, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 June, 1623, B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 10 July, 1628, vicar of Nantglyn, co. Denbigh, 1635, of Ysceifiog, Flints, 1639, and of Mold 1641. See Thomas, 497.
