Loveling, Matthew (Louelin), s. William, of Warren, co. Pembroke, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 June, 1623, aged 20; B.A. 13 Feb., 1625-6, vicar of Warren, 1639; his father vicar of the same 1597. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, Samuel, s. Rice, of Llandovery, co. Carnarvon, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1623, aged 18; B.A. 29 Oct., 1623, M.A. 5 July, 1626, rector of Llanynys, co. Brecon, 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Adams, William, 1s. Nicholas, of Patterchurch, co. Pembroke, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 June, 1623, aged 15.
Meyricke, Roland (Merricke), s. John, of Pembroke (town), gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 June, 1623, aged 15.

Dayrell, Thomas, 2s. Walter, of Abingdon, Berks, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1624, aged 15; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1628, B.A. 18 July, 1628, & M.A. 24 April, 1632, as junior, ejected by the parliamentary visitors 1648, of Gray's Inn 1616, rector of Easington, Oxon, ordered, 11 May, 1647, to be sequestered because he 'has resided at Oxford since it was a garrison for the king,' died 20 Feb., 1654-5, brother of Paul same date and of Walter 1628. See also Thomas Darell, p. 373. See Gutch, i. 296, 303; Burrows; & Add. MS., 15,671, p. 18.

Jessoppe, Constantine, s. John, of Pembroke town, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1624, aged 22; B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin, incorp. 30 June, 1631; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 8 May, 1632, rector of Fyfield, Essex, 1643, and in 1647 for two months; vicar of St. Nicholas, Bristol, Aug. 1647, of Coggeshall, Essex, 1651, rector of Wimborne Minster, Dorset, 1654, until he died 16 April, 1658; father of the next. See Ath. iii. 540; Add. MS. 15,671, f. 80; & D.N.B.

Paule, John, s. John, of Bristol (city), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; B.A. 2 June, 1627, M.A. 15 April, 1630; incorporated at Cambridge 1632, sequestered by the Westminster assembly to the rectory of St. George's Botolph, London, but had returned to his own country before Dec., 1645, ejected from rectory of St. James, Bristol, 1660; perhaps father of the next. See Add. MS. 15,669, p. 229; & Calamy, iii. 177.

Morgan, Lewis, s. Thomas, of Michaelston, co. Glamorgan, militis. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; of Rhiwperra, student of Middle Temple 1625, M.P. Cardiff 1628-9, knighted 25 March, 1629; died at Hampstead 3 July, 1635; probably brother of Walter 1629, and of William 1635. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Wilts Archæological Mag. 1871, xiii. p. 9.

Lloyd, Richard, 4s. John, of Llanrhaiadr, co. Denbigh, militis. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 10 July, 1628, perhaps incorporated at Cambridge 1637; rector of Llanhychan, co. Denbigh, 1633, and of Clocaenog, co. Denbigh, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Thomas, David, 1s. Walter, of Swansea, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; perhaps B.A. 25 June, 1633, and usher or "Ostiarius" of the free school at Thame, and master of schools at Dorchester, and at Leicester, where he died in Aug., 1667. See Ath. i. p. vii.

Prichard, William, s. Richard David, of Abergwilly, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 7 May, 1629; perhaps 2nd master of Westminster school 1626, and vicar of Manerbier, co. Pembroke, 1631. See Al. West. 19; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Edwardes, John, s. John, of Caldecot, co. Monmouth, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 21 Oct., 1626, M.A. 13 June, 1629, rector of Llanmartin 1626, of Wilcrick 1631-2, and of Tredennock (all co. Monmouth) 1633, vicar of Magor, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Langford, Roger, s. Roger, of Ruthin, co. Denbigh, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 10 July, 1628 (possibly of the Inner Temple 1627, as of Lapford, Devon, gen.); his father was of Gray's Inn 1606. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lewis, Eubulus, s. John, of Yale, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 15 April, 1630, vicar of Denbigh 1638, rector of Newtown, co. Montgomery, 1640, until deprived 1646. See Thomas, 344; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, Richard, s. "John Prichard", of Poole, co. Montgom., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 10 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 11 July, 1628, and vicar of Llanfair Caereinion, co. Montgomery, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Edwards, John, s. William, of Bystree (? Yspytty), co. Flint, sacerd. Jesus College, matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 31 Jan., 1626-7; one of these names vicar of Combe (or Cwm), Flints, 1634, vicar of Ysceifog, Flints, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Williams, Henry, s. Maurice, of Llangellor, co. Merioneth, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18, B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 13 June, 1629; vicar of Kenarth 1632, and of Llandissilio, co. Carmarthen, 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bassett, John, s. Thomas, of Llancarvan, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 31 Jan., 1626-7, M.A. 13 June, 1629, one of these names rector of Noseley, co. Leicester, 1638. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Nicholas, Giles, s. Andrew, of Llansey, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 16; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 10 July, 1628 (as Nicholls), rector of Llangunider, co. Brecon, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ellis, John, s. "Ellis Price, of Gwernafeild, co. Flint, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 13 Feb., 1625-6; one of these names vicar of Eastyn or Queenhope, Flints, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hanley, John, s. Christopher, of Knighton, co. Radnor, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 20; B.A. 21 Oct., 1626, rector of Aberporth, co. Cardigan, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 89.

Prosser, Walter, s. "Roger David," of co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; B.A. 21 Oct., 1626, ejected from the rectory of Tredunnoch, co. Monmouth, 1662. See Calamy, iii. 506.

Williams, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Llangunnider, co. Brecon, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19, B.A. 9 Feb. 1625-6; his father rector of Llangunnider 1611. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Watkins, Thomas, s. "Watkin Philips," of Llandetti, Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 20, B.A. 31 Jan., 1626-7; rector of Llansantffraid, co. Brecon, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lewes, John, s. James, of Gwersillt, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 13 Feb., 1625-6; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1634. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Stradling, Edmund, 2s. John, of St. Donatt's, co. Glamorgan, bart. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17, B.A. 1 July, 1624, M.A. 17 April, 1627; died at Oxford; brother of Edward 1615.
Gibbs, John, s. David, of Neath, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1636; brother of William 1622. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Jones, Richard, s. "John Pew", of Henllan, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 21; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 11 July, 1628; died in Ireland. See Ath. iii. 344.
Vaughan, Robert, s. Robert, of Ludlow, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 16; B.A. 2 June, 1627; student of Inner Temple 1624. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Price, Rowland, s. Evan, of Llanfairclydogan, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19; B.A. 21 Oct., 1626, M.A. 31 Oct., 1629.
Williames, Evan, s. "Wm. Prichard," of Bodurog, co. Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19, B.A. 5 July, 1626, M.A. 9 July, 1629.
Birch, James, s. James, of London, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 15; B.A. 23 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 14 May, 1632, B.D. 9 July, 1640.
Williams, Lewis, s. "Wm. Meredith," of Llanbother, co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 20, perhaps B.A. 11 Nov., 1630.
Davies, William, s. 'David Powell,' of Llangunider, co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 20; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6.
Walters, William, s. "Walter Thomas," of Llangunnider, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 20, B.A. 21 Oct., 1626.
Williams, Roderick, s. William, of Conwyl Gaio, co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18, B.A. 21 Oct., 1626.
Griffithes, Isaac, s. William, of Llangevela, co. Glamorgan, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 2 June, 1627.
Evans, Edward, s. "Evan Price, of Llanbadocke, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19; B.A. 21 Oct., 1626.
Feild, Thomas (Field), s. Guy, of London, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 21; B.A. 21 Oct., 1626, M.A. 9 July, 1629.
Price, Francis, s. William, of Llantillio, co. Monmouth, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18, B.A. 8 July, 1626.
Johnes, Thomas, s. John Williams, of Oswestry, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17; B.A. 10 Feb., 1625-6.
Morgan, Walter, s. "Morgan Morris." of Llansawell, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18.
Wynne, John, s. John, of Rhylofneyd, Flints, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17. See also Edward 1675.
Davies, Owen, s. 'David Roberts,' of Llantrissaint, co. Anglesey, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19.
Moston, Samuel, s. Henry, of Calcot, Flints, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18; B.A. 5 July, 1626.
Lewes, Charles, s. "Lewis Prichard," of Llangibby, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 19.
Williams, Francis, s. John, of Pwllcrochon, co. Pembroke, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 22.
Curzonne, Henry, s. Thomas, of Llandyrnogg, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 20.
Morgan, Edward, s. Thomas, of St. Mellan, co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17.
Salusbury, John, s. Edward, of Llandyrnog, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18.
Jones, John, s. William, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 17.
Vaughan, Henry, s. Edward, of Trawscoed, co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 18.
Starkey, Arthur, 1s. Laurence, of Rewbury, Cheshire, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 30 April, 1624, aged 16.

Stratford, Ferdinando, s. John, of Walford, co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 Feb., 1625-6, aged 16, and again 9 March, 1626-7, then aged 19, B.A. 2 June, 1627, M.A. 15 April, 1630; vicar of Tainton, co. Gloucester, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lloyd, Samuel, s. Richard, of Ruabon, co. Denbigh, S.T.D. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 June, 1626, aged 18; B.A. 5 July, 1626, M.A. 11 July, 1628, vicar of Steventon, Berks, 1630, rector of Penegoes, co. Montgomery, 1632, vicar of Gresford, co. Denbigh, 1635-73, until his death in 1673; sinecure rector of Llansannan (2nd portion) 1643, and of Cilcain, Flints, 1644, rector of Aldford, Cheshire, 1663; brother of Humphrey 1628, and of Rice 1632. See Thomas, St. Asaph, 397; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Maddocks, Thomas, s. Lewis, of Llangonwyde, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1626, aged 19; B.A. 5 July, 1626, M.A. 30 April, 1629; perhaps vicar of Marshfield, co. Monmouth, 1640, and rector of Merthyr Dovan, co. Glamorgan, 1640, canon of Llandaff 1660, vicar of St. Llythians, co. Glamorgan, 1661, rector of St. Andrew, co. Glamorgan, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Holland, Adam, s. George, of Purslowe, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1626, aged 18; B.A. 11 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 12 June, 1630, vicar of Long Ashton, Somerset, 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Henry, s. "Rice-Bevan Thomas," of Llanedy, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll. matric. 23 Jan., 1626-7, aged 23; one of these names rector of Llanedi 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Philipps, John, 1s. Richard, of Richmore, co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 March, 1626-7, aged 19, B.A. 2 June, 1627.
Jones, John, s. Thomas, of Llandrinio, co. Montgom., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1626-7, aged 19; B.A. 12 June, 1630.
Crowther, Thomas, s. Francis, of Bishop's Castle, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 June, 1626, aged 13.

Lloyd, Humfrey, s. Richard, of Ruabon, co. Denbigh, doctoris. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17, B.A. from Oriel Coll. 23 Jan., 1629-30; fellow 1631, M.A. 12 May, 1635, created D.D. 12 Sept., 1661; canon of York 1660, vicar of Ruabon 1647 (deprived), and 1660-73 (on his father's death), vicar of Northop, Flints, 1661, cursal canon of St. Asaph 1661, dean 1663, vicar of Gresford 1673, bishop of Bangor with archdeaconries of Bangor and Anglesey 1673-89, canon of Bangor 1676, sinecure rector of Llanrhaider-in-Kinmerch, co. Denbigh, 1679, and of Llandinam, co. Montgomery, 1683; died 18 Jan., 1688-9; brother of Samuel 1626, and of John 1621, father of Francis 1668, and Richard 1673. See Ath. iv. 873; Thomas, 243; Burrows, 536; Fasti, ii. 258; Lansdowne MS. 987, ff. 105, 122; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Dayrell, Walter, s. Walter, of Abingdon, Berks, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; student of Christ Church 1631, B.A. 17 Oct., 1631, M.A. 11 June, 1634, to be created B.D. when he shall please, by order 1 Feb., 1647-8, D.D. 9 Nov., 1660, ejected by the parliamentary visitors 1649, restored 1660, rector of Crawley, Hants, 1662, canon of Winchester 1662, and archdeacon 1666, until his death 29 March, 1684, aged 74. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Fasti, ii. 242; & Burrows.

Powell, Thomas, s. John, of Cantreff, co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18; B.A. 4 Nov., 1629, M.A. 5 June, 1632, D.D. 2 Aug., 1660, rector of Cantreff 1635, "sequestered of his spiritualities" in the time of the civil war, canon of St. Davids at his death 31 Dec., 1660; buried in St. Dunstan's-in-the-West (his father rector of Cantreff 1601-26); father of Francis 1667. See Ath. iii. 507; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Westfalinge, James, s. Herbert, of Hampton, co. Hereford, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Jan., 1627-8, aged 22, B.A. 11 Feb., 1627-8; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 2 Dec., 1630; vicar of Llanover, co. Monmouth, 1629, of Walford 1634. and of Lynton and Lee, (both) co. Hereford, 1637, until sequestered in 1647. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p.9.

Gwyn, Rowland, 1s. Roderick, of Llanbrenin, co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 10 July, 1629, M.A. 12 May, 1632; student of Middle Temple 12 Nov., 1629 (his father of Glanbrane); buried in the choir of the Temple church 3 Dec. following. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lloyd, Rowland, s. David, of Cerrigmawra, co. Monensis (Cerrigceinwen, co. Anglesey), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Jan., 1627-8, aged 23; B.A. 23 Feb., 1627-8; perhaps rector of Llanllyfini, co. Carnarvon, 1661, of Llaneilian, als. St. Hillary. 1661, and of Llangefni, co. Anglesey, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Williams, Jenkin, s. Jenkin, of Cowbridge, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 16, B.A. 22 Nov., 1631, M.A. 14 June, 1634; vicar of Llancarvan 1641, rector of Llantrithyd 1661, and of St. Nicholas, (all) co. Glamorgan, 1663, precentor of Llandaff 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Roberts, Edward, s. Thomas, of Llanbedr, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 11 Feb., 1631-2; perhaps vicar of Llangollen, co. Denbigh, 1648, and of Llansantffraid-yn-Mechan, co. Montgomery, 1661. See Thomas, 510; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Foxwist, William, 1s. Richard, of Carnarvon town, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; bar.-at-law, of Lincoln's Inn, 1636, bencher 1649, M.P. Carnarvon Jan., 1647, till secluded 1648, co. Anglesea 1654-5, Swansea 1659, St. Albans 1660. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Watkins, John, s. John, of Llanspythett, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19, B.A. 18 June, 1631, M.A. 24 April, 1634; prebendary of St. David's 1631, and rector of Penderyn, Brecon, 1638. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Smithe, William (Smythe), s. William, of Llandyrnock, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; perhaps B.A. from Wadham Coll. 26 June, 1630, and vicar of Wrexham, co. Denbigh, 1661; father of William 1669.

Prichard, William, s. Francis, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; B.A. 2 Dec., 1630, M.A. 27 June, 1633, vicar of Little Tinterne, co. Monmouth, 1632-9. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Williams, Robert, 2s. Henry, of Gwernevatt, co. Brecon, militis. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1638; brother of John 1642, and of Millborn 1634. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Cecill, Thomas, s. William, of Monmouth (town), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1627, aged 19; B.A. 24 Oct., 1628, M.A. 18 June, 1631, rector of Llanbedr, co. Brecon, 1639. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Powell, Hierom (Jerome), s. Meredith, of Masemore, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19, B.A. 8 July, 1629; vicar of Llandevalley, co. Brecon, 3 Dec., 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Davies, Evan, s. David, of Llanspythid, co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19; perhaps vicar of St. David's alias Llanvaes, co. Brecon, 1638. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Griffith, Thomas, s. "Griffin Davie", of Llangspy, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19; B.A. from Christ Church 23 Jan., 1629-30, perhaps M.A. from Jesus Coll. 10 July, 1635.

Deere, William, s. Edward, of Llangunian (parish of Llanblethian), co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1636. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Evans, Robert, s. "Evan Price", of Bodvari, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 21; B.A. 14 Feb., 1628-9, possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1631. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Williams, William, s. David, of Pethouse (Bettws), co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; perhaps vicar of Kenarth, co. Carmarthen, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Griffin (Prise), s. Evan, of Glaywern, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 22; perhaps vicar of Llanwnen, co. Cardigan, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cacott, John, s. John, of Sundrich, Kent, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; one of these names rector of Corringham, Essex, 1660. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Roberts, John, s. "Robt. Jeffreys," of Llanworog, co. Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19; B.A. 10 July, 1628, M.A. 10 May, 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lewis, Thomas, s. Edward, of Pontwill, co. Brecknock, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18; rector of Llanvigan, co. Brecon, 1635, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Maurice, Rice, s. Morris Griffith, of Llanbagrene, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19; B.A. 17 Feb., 1630-1, M.A. 22 Oct., 1633, as Morreys.
Morgans, Thomas, s. Howell, of Oxford, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 15; B.A. 16 Jan., 1629-30; perhaps M.A. from Gloucester Hall 20 April, 1638.
Van, John, 1s. Edward, of Marcrosse, co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1627. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Bishop, William (Bushopp), s. John, of Newbirgh, co. Stafford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17; B.A. 5 July, 1631, M.A. 11 June, 1634.
Catchmay, Christopher, s. John, of Trelleck, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1627, aged 21; B.A. 11 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 12 June, 1630.
Griffith, Rees, s. William, of Llanvais, Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18; B.A. 30 April, 1631, M.A. 21 Jan., 1633-4.
Lloyd, Henry, s. John, of Llanbedr, co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 20; B.A. 27 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 14 May, 1632.
Williames, Owen, s. "William Hughes," of Botheterne, co. Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18, B.A. 17 Feb., 1630-1.
Williams, Hamphrey, s. William Thomas, of Glyn, co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Jan., 1627-8, aged 21, B.A. 11 Feb., 1627-8.
Davies, Peter, s. 'David Lewis,' of Llanynnis, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 21; B.A. 12 June, 1630.
Williams, Owen, s. William, of Boturdin, co. Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Jan., 1627-8, aged 23, B.A. 11 Feb., 1627-8.
Thomas, Henry, s. William, of Carnarvon (town), militis. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19, B.A. 11 Feb., 1627-8.
Thomas, Hugh, s. "Thomas Hughes," of "Taylaycavan," co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18.
Jenkins, Lewelin, s. "Jenkin Lewis", of Glynneath, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18.
Meredith, William, s. Meredith Jones, of Llanpeter, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19.
Henman, John, s. Christopher, of Cowbridge, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 17.
Herbert, William, s. John, of Pennarth, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 20.
Lloyd, John, s. Thomas, of Llangunllo, co. Cardigan, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18.
Jones, Richard, s. Alexander, of Carmarthen town, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 18.
Powell, John, s. Evan, of Presteign, co. Radnor, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 19.
Andrewes, John, s. Robert, of Hereford (city), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1627-8, aged 22.

Rogers, Walter, s. Thomas, of Hereford (city), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 May, 1628, aged 21; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 7 Feb., 1628-9, M.A. from Jesus Coll. 17 Oct., 1631; rector of Mordiford 1635, canon of Hereford 1660, and vicar of Fownhope, co. Hereford, 1670, until his death in 1690. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bassett, Edmund, s. William, of Miskin, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1628, aged 20; B.A. 24 Oct., 1628, hanged for killing his wife, who was a daughter of Edward Carne, of Nash; brother of William 1632.

Lloyd, Robert, s. Robert, of Chirk, co. Denbigh. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 June, 1628, aged 19; B.A. 11 July, 1628; perhaps vicar of Bettws-yn-Rhos 1628, and rector of Llanhychan, co. Denbigh, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Tanat, Edward, s. Griffin, of Glan Tanat, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 April, 1628, aged 22, vicar of Llanyblodwell, Salop, 1629, and of Llansillin, co. Denbigh, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Thomas, 519.
Griffith, Thomas, s. David, of Llanllowhere, co. Cardigan, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 May, 1628, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 1 Feb., 1630-1, M.A. 15 Nov., 1633.
Cadogan, Thomas, s. Alexander, of Llangattock, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1628, aged 20; B.A. 24 Oct., 1628.
Powell, William, s. Henry, of Conwyl-Gayo, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 June, 1628, aged 20; B.A. 10 July, 1628.
Owens, John, s. "Owen Griffin," of Llanegwad, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 May, 1628, aged 16.
Thomas, Morgan, s. Thomas, of Llanbeder, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 May, 1628, aged 19.
Gardner, Richard, s. Roger, of Stretton-super-Fosse, co. Warwick, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 May, 1629, aged 24; B.A. 9 May, 1633.
Rice, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 May, 1629, aged 31; "in civitate West Chestriæ natus."

Jones, William, s. Antony, of Carmarthen town, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1630, aged 21; B.A. 26 Oct., 1630, M.A. 4 July, 1633; perhaps rector of Kilymaenllyd, co. Carmarthen, ejected 1662 for nonconformity. See Calamy, iii. 497.
Richard, David, s. "Lewis David," of Penmayne, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 June, 1630, aged 15; B.A. 25 June. 1633, M.A. 3 May, 1636.
Price, Bartholomew (Prise), s. Thomas, of Brecknock, co. Brecon, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Dec., 1630, aged 16; B.A. 1 Feb., 1633-4.

Kemeys, (Sir) Charles, 1s. Nicholas, of Llanveyrtis, co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 17; of Cefn Mably, co. Glamorgan, student of Gray's Inn 1634, knighted at Oxford 13 June, 1643, 2nd bart. on the death of his father (1646), sheriff co. Glamorgan 1644; died 1658; father of the next. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Nicholls, Henry, s. David, of Cowbridge, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19; B.A. 30 Jan., 1634-5; perhaps vicar of Cadoxton-juxta-Neath, 1660, and rector of Neath 1667, rector of St. Brides with Michaelstonsuper-Ely, all co. Glamorgan, 1672-89; father of the next. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lloyd, John, 4s. Jenkin, of Birthlloyd, co. Montgomery, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19 (subs. as Floyd); B.A. 21 June, 1633, M.A. 3 May, 1636; perhaps of All Souls' Coll., D.D. 2 Aug., 1660, "chaplain to the king in his exile," canon of Windsor 18 July, 1660, until his death 9 April, 1671. See Fasti, ii. 236.

Croft, Thomas, s. Alexander of Carmarthen (town), arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1631, aged 18, B.A. 22 Nov., 1631; fellow of All Souls' Coll. 1634, expelled 1648, restored 1658, M.A. 4 June, 1635, D.C.L. 3 July, 1661, of Gray's Inn 1634. See Burrows, 194; & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Evans, Daniel, s. David, of Llanfarian, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 18; B.A. 21 June, 1633, M.A. 3 May, 1636, created B.D. 1 (or 2) Nov., 1642, rector of Llanfrynach 1641, and of Burton, co. Pembroke, 1643. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Edwards, Jonathan, s. John, of Wrexham, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 18; B.A. 9 June, 1634, fellow, M.A. 24 April 1637, created B.D. 1 (or 2) Nov., 1642, rector of Cathedine, co. Brecon. See Fasti, ii. 36; & Calamy, iii. 495.

Price, David, s. Walter, of Pencarrock, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19, B.A. 2 July, 1634; perhaps ejected from the vicarage of Llangunnor, co. Carmarthen, in 1662, for nonconformity. See Calamy, iii. 497.

Johnes, Richard (Johnes), s. Rice, of Llanlloney, co. Carmarth., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 21; B.A. 7 June, 1632, M.A. 30 April, 1635; perhaps vicar of Treleck-ar-Bettws, co. Carmarthen, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Martin, Thomas, s. Richard, of Winchester, Hants, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 Aug., 1631. aged 16; B.A. from New Coll. 20 Oct., 1635, M.A. 13 June, 1638; rector of Winchester St. Michael 1640, See Foster's Index Eccl.
Holland, John, s. John, of Cradley, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 18; B.A. 1 Feb., 1633-4; perhaps rector of Sausthorpe, co. Lincoln, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Floyd, John, 4s. Jenkin, of Birthlloyd, co. Montgomery, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19, as Lloyd; B.A. 21 June, 1633, M.A. 3 May, 1636 (as Lloyd, which see).
Lewis, Hugh, s. Morgan, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1642. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Vaughan, John, 1s. Henry, of Derwith, co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1633. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Burges, John, s. Richard, of Witney, Oxon, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 16; B.A. 21 June, 1633, M.A. 3 May, 1636, brother of Richard 1640.
Price, John, s. John, of Caerwys, Flints, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 22 April, 1634, perhaps identical with.
Davies, Robert, s. John, of Masbrooke, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1631, aged 20 (called Davis in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 27 Oct., 1631.
Jones, Robert, s. John, of Llangaven, co. Carmarth., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 20; B.A. 25 Oct., 1632, M.A. 4 June, 1635.
Jones, Rice, s. John, of Llangawer, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19; B.A. 1 Feb., 1633-4, M.A. 27 Oct., 1636.
Hughes, James, s. William, of Llangathen, co. Carmarthen, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 19; B.A. 1 June, 1633.
Jones, George, s. George, of Abercothye, co. Carmarth., gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 17.
Roberts, John, s. Roger, of Dingestow, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, aged 17.
Price, Thomas, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2, B.A. 7 June, 1632.
Jones, Richard, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1631-2.

Rolles, Richard, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 March, 1632-3, B.A. next day, M.A. 15 Oct., 1635; perhaps created B.D. 20 Dec. 1642, "ex regis gratia," rector of Wavendon, Bucks, and of Witham, Essex, 1646, by the Westminster assembly. See Add. MS. 15,670, p. 70.
Jones, Thomas, pleb., of Jesus Coll., matric. 1 March, 1632-3, B.A. next day.
Lloyd, William, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 March, 1632-3, B.A. next day.

Phillipes, William, s. Philip, of Llanguge, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 April, 1633, aged 21; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 21 June, 1633; one of these names rector of Kilybebil, co. Glamorgan, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Walter, Henry, s. John, of Piersfield, co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 April, 1633, aged 22, B.C.L. 22 Oct., 1633; vicar of Newport, co. Monmouth, ejected for nonconformity in 1662. See Calamy, iii. 506.

Brabourn, William, s. Robert, of Monmouth (town), sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 March, 1634-5, aged 15; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 24 Nov., 1638; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 2 July, 1641, rector of Newington with Brightwell, Oxon, 1663 (then D.D.), canon of St. Paul's 1660, and of Hereford 1660, precentor 1682, until his death 10 March, 1684-5. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: D.D. 24 Dec., 1660, chaplain to the archbishop of Canterbury.]

Jones, Edmond, s. John, of Llandenny, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 21; B.A. 30 Jan., 1635-6; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1641, treasurer 1671, attorney-general S. Wales 1659, recorder of Brecknock and Carmarthen 1660, M.P. co. Brecon 1654-5 and 1659, until disabled 12 Feb., 1659; died 1683, aged 71. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

James, Lewis, s. Lewis, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 16; B.A. 15 Oct., 1635, M.A. 2 June, 1638, rector of Llanhileth, and of Panteague, co. Monmouth, 1661, and of Llanvetherine (all co. Monmouth) 1676, canon of Llandaff 1670. See Edward 1683. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Thomas, Robert, s. "Thos. Jacob," of St. Brides, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1634, aged 18; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 23 June, 1637; student of Gray's Inn 1637; one of these names rector of Gelligaer, co. Glamorgan, 1662. See also Calamy, iii. 505; & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

James, David, s. James, of Kennorth, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Aug., 1634, aged 19; B.A. 15 June, 1637, M.A. 2 July, 1641, rector of Kilrhedin, co. Carmarthen, 1660, vicar of Llanfynydd, co. Carmarthen, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Deere, Thomas (Deare), s. Edward, of Llanblethian, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 Aug., 1634, aged 18; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 15 June, 1637, rector of Llanilid alias St. Juliet, co. Glamorgan, 1660. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bassett, William, s. William, of Beaupré, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 18; B.A. 15 June, 1637, one of these names rector of Llandow, co. Glamorgan. 1660, father of John 1672. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Curtler, John, s. William, of Knighton, Radnor, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Aug., 1634, aged 18 (called Cuttler in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 6 May, 1637. rector of Bishopstone, co. Hereford, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Smith, Humphrey, s. John, of Edwin Ralphe, co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 17, B.A. 3 July, 1636; one of these names rector of Penderyn, co. Brecon, and father of John 1683.
Sterrell, Robert, s. Henry, of London, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Sept. 1634, aged 22; B.C.L. from Gloucester Hall 2 May, 1640; rector of Little Wigborough, Essex, 1642. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lewes, Thomas, s. William, of Carmarthen (town), gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 18; B.A. 15 June, 1637, M.A. 2 May, 1640, student of Gray's Inn 1634. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Williams, Millborn, born in Brecknock, s. Henry, of Gwernevet, co. Radnor, militis. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Sept., 1634, aged 17, B.A. 9 June, 1635; brother of John 1642, and of Robert 1628.
Hickes, Francis, s. William, of Wootton-underEdge, co. Gloucester, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 July, 1634, aged 18, B.A. 15 Oct., 1635, M.A. 2 June, 1638.
Roberts, Morris, s. "Robert Jones," of St. Michel, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 18; B.A. 12 Oct., 1637, M.A. 1 July, 1641.
Walter, William, s. John, of St. Athans, co. Monmouth, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 19, B.A. 15 Oct., 1635, M.A. 13 June, 1638.
Mansell, Antony, s. Richard, of St. Ismael, co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 19; B.A. 17 Dec., 1635, as Maunsell.
Owens, Elias, s. Evan, of Beguildy, co. Radnor, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 19; B.A. 6 May, 1637, M.A. 23 Jan., 1639-40.
Jones, William, s. "John Edwards", of Mallwyd, co. Montgom., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 18; B.A. 18 Feb., 1636-7.
Prichard, William, born in Anglesea, s. Richard Jones, of Trestraith, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Oct., 1634, aged 18.
Williams, Thomas, s. Daniel, of Abercamlais, co. Brecon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Sept., 1634, aged 20; brother of John 1635.
Jeffreys, Howell, s. Edward, of Llywell, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 17; B.A. 20 Jan., 1637-8.
Penry, Rowland, s. Meredith, of Llangammarch, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 May, 1634, aged 20; B.A. 9 June, 1634.
Lewys, Lewis, s. Richard, of St. Mary's, Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 17; B.A. 15 Dec., 1637.
Wynne, John, s. Cadwallader, of Cerrig-y-Druidion, co. Denbigh, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 18.
Williams, William, s. Thomas, of Llanvanin, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 19.
Thomas, John, s. George, of Llantrissent, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Sept., 1634, aged 18.
Vaughan, Hugh, s. David, of Llanwadern, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Aug., 1634, aged 17.
Woods, Howell, s. Owen, of St. Quiven, Anglesey, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Sept., 1634, aged 19.
Jones, George, s. Lewis, of Monmouth town, pleb. Jesus Coll. matric. 23 Sept., 1634, aged 18.

Lloyd, William, s. Thomas, of Woodford, Northants, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1635, aged 15; B.A. 17 Oct., 1638, M.A. 2 July, 1641; perhaps vicar of East Stoke, Notts, 1642, and sequestered to the rectory of Woodford, Northants (2 moieties), 1645, by the Westminster assembly, but ejected for nonconformity in 1662. See Add. MS. 15,669, p. 52; Calamy, iii. 50; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, Edward, s. Gilbert, of Poole, co. Montgom., gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1635, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1635; one of these names vicar of Machynlleth, co. Montgomery, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Gray's Inn Reg.

Lewys, William, s. Richard, of St. Maries, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 May, 1635, aged 17; B.A. from Hart Hall 3 Feb., 1637-8, M.A. 6 March, 1640-1; rector of Portskewett, co. Monmouth, 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gwyn, David, 2s. Thomas, of Hay, co. Brecknock, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1635, aged 16; of the Hay, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1647; buried in the Temple church 27 Jan., 1637-8. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bevan, Nicholas, s. Roger, of Haverfordwest, co. Pembroke, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 May, 1635, aged 18; B.A. 17 Oct., 1638, rector of Hasguard, co. Pembroke, 1642. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Gwyn, Howell, 1s. Thomas, of Hay, co. Brecknock, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1635, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1637. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Feild, Theophilus, born in Monmouth, s. Theophilus, bishop of St. Davids. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1635, aged 14; B.A. 17 Oct., 1638, M.A. 2 July, 1641.
Lloyd, Thomas, s. Hugh, of Narberth, co. Pembroke, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 July, 1635; his father rector of Narberth 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, Thomas, s. "John Evans", of Llanganten, co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 May, 1635, aged 18; B.A. 17 Oct., 1638.
Selby, James, s. Roland, of Llandewy-Brefi, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 May, 1635, aged 17; B.A. 25 Feb., 1638-9.
Howell, Robert (Howle), s. Edward, of Cascobb, co. Radnor, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 July, 1635, aged 18.
Thomas, Thomas, s. Walter, of Swansea, co. Glamorgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 July, 1635, aged 16.

Hughes, Oliver, s. John, of Kingsland, co. Hereford, S.T.D. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 May, 1636, aged 17; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 4 July, 1639, rector of Humber, co. Hereford, 1664. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Jones, Morgan, s. "John David", of Trevethin, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 18; B.A. 12 Dec., 1639, vicar of Undy, co. Monmouth, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hughes, William, s. John, of Kingsland, co. Hereford, S.T.D. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 May, 1636, aged 16; B.A. 14 Nov., 1639, M.A. 15 June, 1642.
Lewis, John, s. John, of Llysnewydd, co, Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 18; B.A. 27 June, 1639, M.A. 15 June, 1642.
Davies, Owen, s. 'David Morgans,' of Llanddeinolen, co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 16; B.A. 11 Feb., 1639-40.
Wynne, Owen, s. John, of Llanvair, co. Denbigh, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 18, B.A. 14 Nov., 1639, M.A. 15 June, 1642.
Jones, Richard (Johnes), s. "John Prichard", of Llanbedrog, co. Carnarv., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 April, 1636, aged 18.
Prichard, Rice, s. Richard, of Llanymtheverye, co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 18.
Stedman, Henry, s. John, of Lewis Lodge, co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 16.
Edwards, Richard, s. Edward, of Bangor, co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1636, aged 18.
Lloyde, Henry, s. John, of Chester (city), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 1 June, 1636, aged 15.

Collins, John, s. Roger, of Ismaels, co. Carmarthen, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1637-8, aged 17; B.A. 9 Dec., 1641, cursal canon or prebendary of St. Asaph 1662, and preb. of Llanddeilo-Graban in Collegiate Church of Brecon (St. David's) 1665, rector of Ystradgynlais, Brecon, 1666. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Briscoe, Nazarite, s. John, of Crofton, Cumberland, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1637, aged 16; called Nazareth in the Heralds' Visitation of 1665, then living in Wales, brother of William 1623.
Boulton, Humfrey, s. Richard, of Beaumaris, co. Anglesea, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 May, 1637, aged 15; B.A. 6 Nov., 1640, M.A. 7 July, 1643. See Burrows.
Winter, Walter, s. Richard, of Llangoed, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 May, 1637, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 21 Nov., 1640.
Westheade, Richard, s. Thomas, of Ormskirk, co. Lancaster, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 May, 1637, aged 18, B.A. 26 Jan., 1640-1.
Jones, William, s. Robert, of Builtb (Puyllt), co. Brecknock, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 May, 1637, aged 17.
Harris, Daniel, s. Roland, of Coyte, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1637-8, aged 17.

Stradling, George, 4s. John, of St. Donatt's Castle, co. Glamorgan, equitis. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 April, 1638, aged 16, B.A. 6 Nov., 1640; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1642, M.A. 26 Jan., 1646-7.; created D.D. 6 Nov., 1661, "an admirable lutinist," chaplain to Dr. Sheldon, bishop of London, rector of Fulham 1660-88, of Hanwell, Middlesex, 1662-4, vicar of Cliffe-at-Hoo 1663, and of Sutton-at-Hone, Kent, 1666, vicar of St. Bride's, London, 1672-3, canon of St. Paul's 1660, and of Westminster 1663, chantor 1671, and dean of Chichester 1672, until his death 19 April, 1688, buried in Westminster abbey 24th; brother of Edward 1615, Edmund, 1624, and Thomas 1615, and father of Gilbert. See Ath. i. p. xxv, and iv. 237; Burrows, 475; West. Abbey Reg. 220; Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 99; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Littleton, (Sir) Thomas, s. Adam, of Stoke, Salop, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1638, aged 16; of Stoke Milburgh, Salop, and of North Ockendon, Essex, 2nd baronet, "Pointz alias Littleton," bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1642; licenced 6 Oct., 1637 (then aged 17), to marry Anne, daughter of Sir Edward Littleton, solicitorgeneral (then aged 12); M.P. Much Wenlock 1661-78, East Grinstead April-July 1679, Yarmouth (Isle of Wight) Feb., and March, 1681; buried at North Ockendon 16 April, 1681; brother of William 1638 and of Edward 1641; father of the next and of Edward 1660. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Herbert, James, s. Philip, Earl of Pembroke. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1638, aged 15; created M.A. 12 April, 1648; of Kinsey, Oxford, M.P. Wilts, May, 1646, till secluded Dec., 1648, Queenborough 1659, 1660, and 1661, until his death in April, 1676; brother of Charles 1632, of John 1638, of William 1635, and of Philip. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Vaughan, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Llansanffraid, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1638, aged 16, B.A. 18 Feb., 1641-2, and fellow; rector of St. Bridgets, Brecknock, a Rosiecrucian; died 27 Feb., 1665, buried at Albury, Oxon, 1 March following; brother of Henry, the Silurist, who died 29 April, 1665. See Ath. iii. 722, and iv. 425.

Herbert, John, s. Philip, Earl of Pembroke. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1638, aged 13; created M.A. 12 April, 1648; M.P. co. Monmouth, circa Oct., 1646 (L.P.), till secluded in 1648, M.P. Wilton 1659; buried in Westminster Abbey 23 Nov., 1659; brother of Charles 1632, James 1638, and Philip. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Blome, Richard (Bloome), s. John, of 'Marudino,' co. Brecknock, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1638, aged 17; created B.C.L. 1 Nov., 1642, 'a publisher and compiler of some celebrity,' died in 1705. See D.N.B. & Fasti, ii. 12.

Littleton, William, born in Herts, s. Adam, of Stoke, Salop, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1638, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1645; brother of Thomas same date, and of Edward 1641. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bowens, Walter, s. John, of Llanthowell, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1638, aged 16; one of these names vicar of Llanfihangel-Ystrad, co. Cardigan, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Lewis, William, born in Berks, 2s. Edward, of Thean, co. Glamorg., equitis. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 12 Oct., 1638, aged 14; his father perhaps knighted 7 May, 1603; brother of Edward 1638.
Floyd, Thomas (Floid), s. Edward, of Mold, co. Flint, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 July, 1638, aged 17; possibly a student of Middle Temple 1638, as Lloyd. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Phillipps, John, s. Rice, of Disserth, co. Radnor, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 July, 1638, aged 20; one of these names rector of Disserth 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Morgan, Humphrey, s. "Morgan Evans," of Crinant, co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 May, 1638, aged 18; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 2 Dec., 1641.
Lewis, Edward, born in Berks, 1s. Edward, of Thean, co. Glamorgan, equitis. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1638, aged 15; brother of William 1638.
Williams, David, s. "William Davids," of Llanennis, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 March, 1638-9, aged 18, B.A. 2 March, 1643-4.
Davis, Griffin, s. 'David Griffin,' of Llangadock, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1638, aged 17; B.A. 25 June, 1641.
Thomas, Richard, s. "Thomas Richards," of Whitchurch, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1638, aged 18, "servitor."
Thomas, John, s. Thomas Charles, of Berrio, co. Montgomery, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 April, 1638, aged 16, B.A. 21 Oct., 1641.
Chapman, Charles, born in co. Gloucester, s. Robert, of Ross, co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 March, 1638-9, aged 17.
Watkins, Andrew, s. John, of Llansaintfraid, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1638, aged 17, B.A. 21 June, 1642.
Griffith, Thomas (Griffieths), s. "Griffin Jones", of Lanpeter, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 May, 1638, aged 16.
Poindexter, Philip, s. Thomas, of Jersey, Hants, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1638, aged 16; B.A. 11 Nov., 1641.
Colton, Francis, s. James, of St. Twinnells, co. Pembroke, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1638, aged 18.
Carteret, Edward de, s. Joshua, of the Isle of Jersey, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 June, 1638, aged 17.
Fletcher, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Treboth, Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1638, aged 16.
Howell, John, s. John, of Caerwent, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 May, 1638, aged 16.

Pugh, Henry, s. Robert, of Penmachno, co. Carnarvon, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 Dec., 1639, aged 15, B.A. 2 Dec., 1643; one of these names vicar of Abergele, co. Denbigh, 1662, and of Ysceifog, Flints, 1664. See Burrows, 503; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Davies, Athanasius, s. Edward, of St. Bride's, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 March, 1639-40, aged 19; rector of Tredunnock 1660, and of Kemeys, co. Monmouth, 1661, father of Thomas 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jones, Jenkin, s. John, of Llanthettye, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 29 March, 1639, aged 16; vicar of Cadoxton-juxta-Neath, co. Glamorgan, until ejected 1660; became a Catabaptist. See Calamy, iii. 500.
Gibbon, William, s. William, of Llanharye, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 April, 1639, aged 16; B.A.; M.A. 14 Dec., 1647, as Gibbons.
Robins, John, s. Maurice, of Carnarvon (town), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1639-40, aged 14, B.A. 2 March, 1643-4, M.A. 18 March, 1646-7.
Phillipps, John, s. James, of Skenfrith, co. Monmouth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1639, aged 19, B.A. 5 May, 1642.
Evans, Roland, s. "Evan Griffiths, of Llanrhisted, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1639-40, aged 18.
Meyrick, Rice (Merick), s. William, of Pendleton, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 April, 1639, aged 17.
Jenkins, David, s. Robert, of Monck-Nash, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1639-40, aged 20.
Thomas, Lewis, s. "Tho. John," of Llanlline, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 April, 1639, aged 18.
Hughes, Richard, s. Hugh, of Festiniog, co. Merioneth, pleb. pp. Jesus Coll., matric. 6 Dec., 1639, aged 17.
Jones, Thomas, s. William, of Rockfeild, co. Monmouth, pleb. pp. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1639, aged 17.
Gwynn, Edward, s. Antony, of Llansannor, co. Glamorgan, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 June, 1639, aged 18.
Jones, Hugh, s. Lewis, of Bettws, co. Denbigh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 April, 1639, aged 15.

Lloyd, Jenkin, s. John, of Varedreff, co. Cardigan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1640, aged 16; created M.A. 14 April, 1648, created D.D. 12 Sept., 1661 (rector of Llandissill, co. Cardigan), sequestered to the rectory of Llangoedmore, co. Cardigan, 1646, by the Westminster assembly, vicar of Llanvihangel-Ystrad, co. Cardigan, 1661, rector of Llanychllwyddog, co. Pembroke, 1663. See Ath. iv. 673; Burrows, 504; Fasti, ii. 258; Add. MS. 15,670, p. 225; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Ellis, Thomas, s. Griffin, of Dolbenmaen, co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 June, 1640, aged 15; B.A. 4 May, 1644, M.A. 23 Jan., 1646-7, B.D. 17 Oct., 1661; 'bore arms for the King within the garrison of Oxford'; rector of St. Mary, Dolgelly, 1665; died at Dolbenmaen in April, 1673. See Ath. iii. 992; Burrows, 503; & D.N.B.
Jenkins, Walter, s. Thomas, of Llaninhing (Llanvetherine), co. Monmouth, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 June, 1640, aged 14.
Weston, Theophilus, s. William, of Carmarthen (town), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1640, aged 18, B.A. 6 July, 1644.
Burges, Richard, s. Richard, of Witney, Oxon, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1640, aged 17; brother of John 1632.
Edwards, Hugh, s. Richard, of Lluell, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 May, 1640, aged 17; B.A. 2 Dec., 1643.
Jones, Daniel, s. "John Griffiths", of Llanarthney, co. Carnarv., pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 27 March, 1640, aged 20.
Williams, William, s. Thomas, of Carleon, co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 15 May, 1640, aged 16.
Price, Thomas, s. David, of Llangranog, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 May, 1640, aged 16.

Jenkins, (Sir) Leoline, s. Leoline, of Llanblethian, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 June, 1641, aged 16, fellow 1660, D.C.L. 12 Feb., 1660-1, principal of his college 1661-73; advocate of Doctors' Commons 11 Nov., 1664, judge of high court of admiralty 1668-73, and of prerogative court of Canterbury 1668-85, served in various diplomatic missions, a plenipotentiary to Cologne, knighted 7 Jan., 1669, one of the principal secretaries of state 1680-4, a member of Gray's Inn 1667, M.P. Hythe (Feb.), 1673-8, and for the university 1679, until he died 1 Sept., 1685. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.; Coote's Civilians, 88; Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; Gutch, i. 586; Fasti, ii. 231; & Cates.

Wilkins, Thomas, s. Roger, of Marychurch, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 July, 1641, aged 15; created B.C.L. 12 Sept., 1661; rector of Gelligaer, co. Glamorgan, 1660, canon of Llandaff 1668, rector of Llanmaes, co. Glamorgan, 1668; died 1698. See Fasti, ii. 252; Burrows, 504; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Davis, John, s. William, of Kidwelly, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 June, 1641, aged 16; admitted sizar of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 14 May, 1646, aged 19 (his father a yeoman), born at Kidwelly 25 May, 1625, buried in the church there 22 July, 1693. See Ath., iv. 382; & D.N.B.

Williams, Lewis, s. Maurice, of Llanddgredod, co. Merioneth, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 17, B.A. 24 Nov., 1646, M.A. 31 May, 1649; perhaps rector of Rhoscolyn, co. Anglesey, 1660, or Llanengan, co. Carnarvon, 1663, and canon of Bangor 1667. See Burrows, 504.

Thomas, William, s. John, of Coychurch, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 June, 1641, aged 17; perhaps rector of St. Marychurch, co. Glamorgan, ejected 1662 for nonconformity, kept school at Swansea. See Calamy, iii. 503.
Howell, Benjamin, s. Thomas, of Pencirewin (? Penderyn), co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 June, 1641, aged 17.
Thomas, Morgan, s. "Thos. Jenkins," of Gwernuilig, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Dec., 1641, aged 18.
Button, William, 3s. Thomas, of Cottrell, co. Glamorgan, equitis. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 April, 1641, aged 16.
Bassett, Mansfield, s. John, of Llantrithid, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 Dec., 1641, aged 18.
Phillipps, Roland, s. William, of Coedgaine, co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 4 June, 1641, aged 16.
Williams, Rowland, s. Christopher, of Stowe, co. Monmouth, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 16.
Aster, John, s. Francis, of Swansea, co., Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 April, 1641, aged 16.
Jones, Thomas, s. John, of Tredoyer, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 April, 1641, aged 19.
Lloyd, John, s. John, of Llangeler, co. Carmarthen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 April, 1641, aged 17.
Caunt, Thomas, s. Walter, of Madeley, co. Hereford, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 28 May, 1641, aged 16.
Lewis, Rice, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1641, aged 16.
Penry, James, s. Hugh, of Devynock, co. Brecon, sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 July, 1647, aged 16; expelled by the parliamentary visitors 1648; perhaps father of the next-named. See Burrows, 503.
Gibbon, Whitegift, sacerd. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1647, aged 16; rector of Porthkerry, co. Glamorgan, 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Burrows, 503.
Williams, John, "serv.," s. William Jones, of Llangane, co. Carnarvon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 July, 1647, aged 20.
Quarrell, James, "sacerd. fil." Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1647, aged 17 (subscribes "serv."). See Burrows, 503.
Price, William, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1647, aged 16 (subscribes "ser."). See Burrows, 503.

Price, Owen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 March, 1648-9, scholar 1648, from co. Montgomery, after four years' residence was called to the charge of a school in Wales; M.A. from Christ Church 6 May, 1656, master of Magdalen college school 1657-60; died at Oxford 25 Nov., 1671, buried in church of St. Peter-in-the-East; admon. at Oxford 13 Dec., 1676, son-in-law of John Blagrave, of Merton Coll. 1602, see page 135; father of Thomas 1688. See Ath. iii. 942; Bloxam, iii. 177; & Burrows, 504.

Eyre, Henry, fellow of Jesus Coll. by visitors 1648, B.A. 26 June, 1649; fellow of Merton Coll. 1651, M.A. 18 Nov., 1652; possibly a student of Lincoln's Inn 1647 (as 10s. of Giles, of Whiteparish, Wilts, esq.), recorder of Salisbury and M.P. 1659 and 1660, M.P. Dowton 1675, until his death 1678. See O.H.S. iv. 292; Burrows, 197; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wilcox, Francis, of Emanuel Coll., Cambridge (admitted 15 Nov., 1645); fellow of Jesus Coll. by the parliamentary visitors in 1648; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 1 May, 1649; rector of Powderham, Devon, 1664. See Burrows, 505; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bruce, Edmund, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 March, 1648-9, B.A. 12 July, 1650 (as Brice); fellow All Souls' Coll. 1650, by visitors. See Burrows, 177, 504.
Pipon, Nicholas, Jersey, fellow Jesus Coll. 1648 by the parliamentary visitors. See Burrows, 505.

Parry, Benjamin, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 March, 1650-1, B.A. 10 Feb., 1651-2; Greek reader and fellow Corpus Christi Coll., M.A. 30 May, 1654, B.D. 12 July, 1662, D.D. 9 July, 1670; admitted to Trinity Coll., Dublin, 5 Dec., 1648 (s. Edward, bishop of Killaloe), canon of York 1664-73, sinecure rector of Hope, Flints, 1666-77, and of Llaniestyn 1667, of Godington, Oxon, 1668, and rector of St. Antholin's, London, 1671-4, chaplain to the earl of Essex, lord lieutenant of Ireland; prebendary of Christ Church, Dublin, 1672, dean of Ossory 1674, and of St. Patrick, Dublin, 1675-7, and precentor 1627-8, bishop of Ossory 1678; died same year; brother of John 1651. See Cotton's Fasti Ecc. Hib. ii. 101; Ath. iii. 1172; Burrows, 504; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Pierce, Edward, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1650, B.A. 10 March, 1654-5, M.A. 25 June, 1657; Wood states that he became a servitor of St. John's Coll. (1652), and rector of St. Michael's, Crooked Lane, London, vicar of Duston 1662-3, and rector of Aldwinckle All Saints' 1663, and of Cottesbrooke, Northants, 1663, licenced 15 May, 1666 (then a widower about 33), to marry Elizabeth, niece of Sir John Langham, kt. and bart.; died at Cottesbrooke 2 Sept., 1694, buried in the chancel 4th; father of John 1684. See Ath. iv. 700; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Williams, (Sir) William, doctoris fil. (subscribes "Theolog. fil."). Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1650; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1658, treasurer 1681 (as son Hugh, of Llantrissaint, co. Anglesey, D.D.), K.C. 1689, recorder of Chester 1667-84, and 1687-1700, speaker house of commons 1679, 1681, solicitorgeneral 1685, knighted 11 Dec., 1687, and created a baronet 6 July, 1688, M.P. Chester (June) 1675-81, Montgomery 1685, till void 10 June, 1685, Beaumaris 1689-90, 1695-8; died 11 July, 1700. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; & Ath. iv. 720.

Foulkes, William, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1650; B.A. 17 Oct., 1653, M.A. 20 April, 1659, (as Fulkes,) rector of Llanbrynmair, co. Montgomery, 1661, of Llanfyllin 1661, cursal canon of St. Asaph 1662, rector of Llanvihangel-yn-Gwnfa 1679; father of the next. See Calamy, iii. 290; Thomas, 764; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Powell, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1650, B.A. 29 March, 1653; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1664; possibly identical with Sir John Powell, judge of common pleas 1686-7, 1689-96. See Foss's Judges.
Rowlands, Richard, pauperis. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1650 (subscribes "ser."), B.A. 29 Jan., 1652-3, M.A. 11 Oct., 1655; perhaps rector of Aberfraw, co. Anglesey, 1657. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Davis, Morgan, 'ser.' Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1650; B.A. 10 March, 1654-5, perhaps rector of Cosheston, co. Pembroke, 1663, and of Nash and Upton 1669. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Davis, Thomas, 'cler. fil.' Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1650; B.A. 25 April, 1653, M.A. 1 Feb., 1655-6, incorp. at Cambridge 1665.
Morgan, Thomas, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1650; B.A. 8 April, 1652, M.A. 11 Nov., 1654. See Burrows, 503.
Foulkes, John, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1650, B.A. 17 Oct., 1653, as Fulkes.
Parry, William, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 March, 1650-1. See Burrows, 503.
Prichard, Watkin, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1650, B.A. 16 May, 1653.
Jones, Rice (subs. "Rhesus"), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1650.
Vaughan, Thomas, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 March, 1650-1.
Owen, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1650.

Maurice, David, of Jesus Coll., pleb., matric. 3 June, 1651 (subs. as Morris); B.A. 10 March, 1654-5; M.A. from New Coll. 11 June, 1657; vicar of Llangernyw 1662, and rector of Kegidog St. George, co. Denbigh, 1663, cursal prebendary of St. Asaph 1664, canon 1666, vicar of Llanasaph, Flints, 1666, rector of Gwytherin 1675, vicar of Abergele 1684, of Bettws-yn-Rhos 1684, rector of Llanarmon-in-Yale, all co. Denbigh, 1696, and perhaps of Garthbeibio, co. Montgomery, 1705. See Thomas, St. Asaph, 390; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Barry, Richard, 'equitis fil.' Jesus Coll., matric. 5 Dec., 1651, entered a student of Lincoln's Inn 17 Aug., 1660, as son of Sir James, of city of Dublin, knight, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1666, probably Richard, 2nd Lord Santry, and father of Henry, 3rd lord. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Roberts, Timothy, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1651; B.A. from New Coll. 22 June, 1655, vicar of Barton, Westmorland, 1653, until ejected 1660 (? 1655-62). See Calamy, iii. 354.
Lloyd, David, 'paup.' Jesus Coll., matric. 5 June, 1651, 'ser.,' B.A. 15 March, 1654-5, M.A. 19 May, 1659; one of these names beneficed in co. Glamorgan. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Herbert, Morgan, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Nov., 1651, a student of the Inner Temple 1665, as of Hafod-Newydd, co. Cardigan. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Thomas, William, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 13 May, 1651. See Burrows, 506.
Williams, John, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 5 June, 1651.
Morris, Henry, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 May, 1651.
Lewis, John, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1651.

Wynne, Robert, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1652; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 10 Dec., 1653 (entered as of Lincoln Coll. 12th), subscribed as of Jesus Coll. 7 Nov., 1655, possibly more than one person; one of these names rector of Llanddeniolen 1667, of Llaniestyn, (both) co. Carnarvon, 1673, and rector of Llantrisaint, co. Anglesey, 1681; died 25 Jan., 1679, aged 43; father of Robert 1677. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Penry, Meredith, pleb. Jesus Coll. matric. 3 July, 1652; B.A. 14 Dec., 1655, M.A. 28 June, 1662, rector of Llantsantffraid-with-Llanhamlach, co. Brecon, 1658, vicar of Brecon St. John 1663; father of Thomas 1680. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bulkeley, Thomas, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 2 April, 1652; a student of Gray's Inn 1654 (as son of Thomas, of Baronhill, co. Anglesey, arm.), a royalist captain in the Civil War. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.
Kyffin, Gruffin, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1652, student of Middle Temple 1655, as Gruffith, son and heir of Watkin, of Glascoed, co. Denbigh, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Price, Edward, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 April, 1652, B.A. 8 April, 1652 (as Edmund); one of these names vicar of Stoneleigh, co. Warwick, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 2 April, 1652; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 21 June, 1655. See Thomas, St. Asaph, 724.
Prichard, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 15 July, 1652; perhaps from Merton Coll. See Burrows, 504, 525.
Powell, William, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1652.
Goodwyn, Robert, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 3 July, 1652.
Price, David, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 8 July, 1652.

Roberts, Philips, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653, B.A. 26 Feb., 1656-7, M.A. 8 July, 1659, B.D. 17 July, 1666, rector of Llansantffraid, co. Brecon, 1664, vicar of Darowen, co. Montgomery, 1664, rector of Bodfari, Flints, 1668, and cursal canon of St. Asaph 1670. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Griffith, John, doctoris fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4, student of Gray's Inn 1654 (as son and heir of William, of Llanvaethlu, co. Anglesey, D.C.L.), high sheriff of Anglesey 1690; brother of Francis 1655, and father of Francis 1695. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Taylor, Richard, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653, B.A. 26 Feb., 1656-7, M.A. 21 June, 1659; perhaps curate of Holt, co. Denbigh, 1659, ejected 1662, became nonconformist minister at Barking, Essex, where he died about 1697. See Calamy, iii. 478.

Christopher, Jenkin, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4, B.A. 12 June, 1657; M.A. from New Coll. 1660, rector of St. Fagan's, co. Glamorgan, 1667, precentor (with preb.) of Llandaff 1671. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Lloyd, Maurice, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4; one of these names vicar of Bucknell, Salop, 1674, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rowlands, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4, B.A. 9 Feb., 1657-8, rector of Llansadwrn, co. Anglesey, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hughes, Hugh, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4.
Vaughan, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4.
Nicholas, Robert, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653.
Powell, Edward, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653.
Lloyd, Edward, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653.
Thomas, Hugh, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653.

Glynne, William, 1s. (John), arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 25 March, 1656, a student of Lincoln's Inn 1652 (his father serjeant-at-law), M.P. co. Carnarvon 1659 and 1660, created a baronet 20 May, 1661, high sheriff Oxon 1668; died about 1690; brother of John 1666, father of William 1679. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; & Fasti, ii. 192.

Parry, David, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 22 April, 1658, vicar of Syston, co. Leicester, 1660-72; and perhaps vicar of Llanrhaiadr-in-Mochnant, co. Denbigh, 1670, rector of Llanvapley, co. Monmouth, 1673, and vicar of Llanfihangel-ararth, co. Carmarthen. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wynne, Owen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654; possibly bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1664, bencher 1683 (as 2s. William, late of Melay, co. Denbigh, esq. deceased); serjeant-at-law 1684, M.P. New Radnor 1685-7. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bowen, James, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654; B.A. 22 April, 1658, M.A. 15 March, 1660-1, rector of Ludchurch, co. Pembroke, 1669, and of Rudbaxton 1671, canon of St. David's 1688. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hughes, Herbert, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 13 May, 1659, rector of Nicholaston 1661, and of Oxwich, co. Glamorgan, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyd, David, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 22 April, 1658, M.A. 25 June, 1661; one of these names beneficed in Anglesea. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gore, Thomas, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654. This name undecipherable in subs. book, but more like "Howe" than Gore.
Llewelyn, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 1661, created M.A. 12 Sept., 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Griffith, Gabriel (Gruffithes), "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654.
Jones, Richard (Johnes), "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654.
Griffiths, Hugh, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654.
Thomas, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654.

Binner, William (Bynner), pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; B.A. 20 Jan., 1658-9, incorp. at Cambridge 1664, as Bynner, one of these names vicar choral of St. Asaph, 1661, rector of Cemmes, co. Montgomery, 1668. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Griffith, Francis, doctoris fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655, B.A. 22 April, 1658, M.A. 16 Jan., 1660-1 (s. William 1615), rector of Llanfihangel-yGwent, co. Montgomery, 1667; brother of John 1654. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, Thedore, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; B.A. 23 March, 1660-1, vicar-choral St. Asaph 1661, rector of Llanerfyl, co. Montgomery, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Foulkes, John, "serv." Jesus Coll., matric. 28 March, 1655; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 19 Oct., 1658, M.A. 2 July, 1661. See Thomas, 695, 732.
Hugh(e)s, Owen, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; B.A. from Christ Church 5 April, 1660.
Bird, William (Byrd), gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Jackson, Thomas, of Jesus Coll., arm., matric. 28 March, 1655.
Roberts, Griffith, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655.
Edisbury, Kenrick, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655.
Walter, Thomas, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 28 March, 1655.
Hutton, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655.

Meyricke, Edmund (Myricke), gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, B.A. 12 Oct., 1659, M.A. 1662, incorporated at Cambridge 1663, vicar of Eynsham, Oxon, 1663, of Llangarthen 1664, and of Llanarthney 1666, and rector of Penboyr (all co. Carmarthen) 1668, rector of Burton, co. Pembroke, 1670, prebendary in collegiate church of Brecon 1670, and precentor 1685, rector of Stackpole Elidor, co. Pembroke, 1675, vicar of Llanegwad, co. Carmarthen, 1677, canon of St. Davids 1690, and treasurer 1691. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bevan, Thomas, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. 1660, M.A. 1662, B. & D.D. 1683 (Thomas Bevans rector of Llowes, co. Radnor, 1661, vicar of Llandilo-Vawr, co. Carmarthen, 1666), father of Thomas, 1687. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, ii. 388.

Salesbury, William, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; student of Gray's Inn 1658, as son and heir of Owen, late of Rug, co. Merioneth, esq., deceased; brother of John 1658, and of Gabriel, and father of John and Owen 1681. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Powell, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7, and also 29 Oct., 1657, B.A. 14 May, 1661; incorporated at Cambridge 1664, and M.A. from King's Coll. 1664; incorporated at Oxford 12 July, 1664.
Wrinch, Edward, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; vicar of Caerleon 1661, rector of Llangattock-juxta-Usk 1662, and of Panteague, (all) co. Monmouth, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Thelwall, William, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7, B.A. 1660; student of Gray's Inn 1660, as son of Edward, of Llanbedr, co. Denbigh, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Ravenscrofte, William, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; student of Gray's Inn, 1657, as son and heir of Thomas, of Bangor, co. Denbigh, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Humphreye, Ellis, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; B.A. 4 March, 1660-1 (as "Elisha"); perhaps vicar of Clynnogvawr, co. Carnarvon, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Coytmore, Lewis, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7, B.A. 1660, M.A. 1663, rector of Eglwyssail, Anglesey, 1669. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thomas, David, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 1660, M.A. 1671, B.D. 1671.
Ellis, Richard, "serv." Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 15 June, 1658.
Roberts, Richard, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656.
Jones, Hugh, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7.
Grant, John, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7.
Pembowe, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656.

Lloyd, Owen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, B.A. 1661; of co. Merioneth, admitted a pensioner of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 26 June, 1665 (see Mayor, p. 171), M.A. 1665, incorporated at Oxford 11 July, 1665, rector of Llanfechan, 1670, and of Llanwyddelan, co. Montgomery, 1670. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wynne, Hugh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, B.A. 1661; fellow All Souls' Coll., B.C.L. 1667, D.C.L. 1672; chancellor of St. Asaph 1685-90, the first nonjuror turned out of the university. See Hearne, iii. 53.
Meyricke, Lewis, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1667, bencher 1689, as son of Edmond, of Ulcheldrei, co. Merioneth, esq. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Lewis, Hugh, pauper. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, gent. (subs. "ser."); B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664, perhaps rector of Bishopston, co. Glamorgan, 1670. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Huxley, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1670; possibly son and heir of Francis, of Broseley, Salop, gent.; brother of Thomas 1663.
Lloyd, Thomas, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8.
Hughes, Hugh, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1657.
Moris, Peter, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1657.

Lewis, William, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658, admitted there 18 March, 1657-8; & as son of Howel, of Anglesey, admitted sizar St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 17 Oct., 1659, aged 19, B.A. 1661; fellow Jesus Coll. and M.A. 1665 (incorporated 1669), LL.D. 1679; perhaps rector of Llanvaethlu-with-Llanwrog 1661, and of Rhoscolyn, co. Anglesey, 1670, vicar of Comberton, co. Cambridge, 1679. See Mayor, 143; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Tench, Robert, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9; B.A. from Hart Hall 1665, incorporated at Cambridge 1668, and M.A. from Christ's Coll. 1668; rector of Llanwrin, co. Montgomery, 1676, cursal canon of St. Asaph 1691, and canon 1693-1701; father of Carbery. See Thomas, 251; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Edwardes, Samuel, "serv." Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9, B.A. 1662; M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1665; incorp. 11 July, 1665; one of these names rector of Weston under Lizzard, co. Stafford, 1687, of Donington, Salop, 1703. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Salesbury, Gabriel, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658; bar.-at-law Gray's Inn 1667, and bencher 1688, as 3s. of Owen of Rug, co. Merioneth, esq.; brother of John 1658, and of William 1656. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Mathewes, Manesseh, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658 (subs. Manesses), rector of Port Eynon 1662, vicar of Swansea 1670, and of Llangevelach (all co. Glamorgan) 1677; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Portrey, Richard, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9, B.A. 1663, rector of Llangenith, co. Glamorgan, 1664, of Rhosilly, co. Anglesey, 1665, and of Ystradgynlais, co. Brecon, 1687. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Glynne, Thomas, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1658, possibly bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1670, as son and heir of William, of Plas Newydd, co. Carnarvon, arm., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Salesbury, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658; student of Gray's Inn 1660, as 2s. Owen, of Rug, co. Merioneth, esq.; brother of William 1656, and of Gabriel. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Madrin, Thomas, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658, student of Lincoln's Inn 1659, as son and heir of Thomas, of Madryn, co. Carnarvon, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Griffith, Thomas, doctoris fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1658, a student of Gray's Inn, 1662, as son and heir of George, bishop of St. Asaph. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Kinaston, Edward, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9; perhaps M.P. Shrewsbury 1678-81 and 1685-7, of Albright Lee; died 1693. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Lloyd, Daniel, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1656, as 2s. Daniel, of Llanon, co. Carmarthen, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Otes, Roger, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug, 1658, student of Gray's Inn 1660, as son and heir of Roger, of Llandenny, co. Monmouth, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Weaver, Thomas, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658; student of Inner Temple 1658, as of Morville, Salop, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Matthews, "Michayah,", cler. fil. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 April, 1658, B.A. 22 March, 1658-9, as "Micah."
Edwards, John, "serv." Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1658, B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664.
Lloyd, Thomas, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658, B.A. 29 Jan., 1661-2.
Parry, Robert, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug. 1658, B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664.
Lewis, Howel, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658, B.A. 1661.
Griffithe, Charles, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658.
Hughes, Richard, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658.
Jones, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9.
Stedman, Richard, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658.
Watkins, Samuel, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1658.
Eyton, John, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9.
Jones, George, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 2 July, 1658.
Jones, William, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658.
Thomas, Robert, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658.
Jones, Henry, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 9 Aug., 1658.

Stradlinge, (Sir) Edward, of co. Glamorgan. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660, created M.A. 12 Sept., 1661; of St. Donat's Castle, co. Glamorgan, 4th baronet (s. Sir Edward); licenced 18 Nov., 1667, to marry Elizabeth Hungerford, of Farley Castle, Wilts, spinster; died 5 Sept., 1685; father of Edward next-named. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Franklin, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Carmarthen town, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1660-1, B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667, rector of Shepton Beauchamp, Somerset, 1670, and of Chalton, Hants, 1672; father of Charles and Thomas 1698, of George and James 1702, and of William 1710. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stokes, Nicholas, s. Anthony, of Roche, co. Pembroke, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1660-1, B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667; vicar of Moncton St. Michael and St. Mary 1668, and of Gumfreston, co. Pembroke 1685, brother of John 1665. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Foulkes, Robert, s. Rice, of Llanvyhangel, co. Denbigh, minister. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1660-1; one of these names vicar choral St. Asaph 1665, vicar of Llandrillo-yn-Rhos, als. Dinerth, co. Denbigh, 1666, See Foster's Index Eccl. & Thomas, 551.
Grant, Robert, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660, B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1664, rector of Bishopston, co. Glamorgan, 1666, and of Michaelstowe, 1667. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Nanney, Hugh, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1660; student of Lincoln's Inn 1663, as son and heir of Griffin, of Nanney, co. Merioneth, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Mathewes, Roger, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1660, student of Lincoln's Inn 1663, as son and heir of Robert, of Blodwell, Salop, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Davies, Owen, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660; B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667, perhaps rector of Llangeinwen, co. Anglesey, 1670, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Owen, Hugh, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1660; perhaps a nonconformist preacher in Monmouthshire. See Calamy, iii. 490.
Williams, Thomas, s. William, of St. Nicholas, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 March, 1660-1, aged 20.
Phillipps, Richard, s. Howel, of Dolehaidd, co. Carmarthen, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1660-1, aged 17.
Thomas, Howell, s. Richard Thomas, of Pitcott, co. Glamorgan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1660-1.
Morrys, Thomas, s. Rice, of Hereford (city), sacerd. Jesus Coll., matric. 16 March, 1660-1, aged 16.
Lewis, David, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660, B.A. 1663, M.A. 1666 (as Lewys).
Williams, Humphrey, "serv." Jesus Coll., matric. 6 June, 1660, B.A. 10 March, 1663-4.
Edwards, Richard, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1660, B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664.
Hughes, Samuel, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660, B.A. 1663, M.A. 1666.
Price, William, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660, aged 19.
Owens, William, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.
Prichard, Owen, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660.
Vaughan, Walter, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 July, 1660.
Jones, Samuel, gent. Jesus Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660.
Owens, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660.
Butler, John, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.

Roberts, Michael, s. Rob., "Cadwallader," of Clynnock, pleb., p.p. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 17; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1665; incorporated at Cambridge 1673, and M.A. from Jesus Coll. 1673, vicar of King's Langley, Herts, 1687. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stokes, George, s. Anthony, of Rocke, co. Pembroke, pleb. p.p. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1661, aged 18, B.A. 1665; M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1680; rector of Ovingdean, Sussex, 1680-1724; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lewes, Morgan, s. William, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, pleb. pp. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 March, 1661-2, aged 17; B.A. 1665, M.A. 1668 (as Lewis), vicar of Caldicott 1669, rector of Llanwern, co. Monmouth, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ducas, (or Duras in Wood), Godefridus de, 'fil. Guy Aldenii Marchionis de Ducas apud Aquitanos.' Jesus Coll., matric. 15 Jan., 1661-2, aged 16; created M.A. 2 March, 1662-3, as Earl of Montgomery. See Fasti, ii. 263.

Williams, Rice, s. Nicholas, of Rydodin, co. Carmarthen, arm. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 July, 1661, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1660; brother of Marmaduke 1668, and of Nicholas 1669. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Hawkins, Philip s. Peter, of St. George's, co. Glamorgan, pleb. pp. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 18; rector of Merthyr Dovan, co. Glamorgan, 1669, and of Wenvoe 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Woods, Paul, s. John, of Flint (town), pleb., p.p. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 17, B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669; vicar of Over Cheshire 1681, as Wood; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Richards, Herbert, s. Robert, of Bridgnorth, Salop, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 May, 1661, aged 18; B.A. 3 Feb., 1664-5, M.A. 1667, rector of Aldington, Kent, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wood, Edward, s. James, of Llanhamlach, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 16; vicar of Llanspythid, co. Brecon, 1668; father of Jacob 1689. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, St. John, gent., of Jesus Coll., matric. 30 May, 1661, student of Gray's Inn 1660, as son and heir of William, of Bridgwater, Somerset, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Williams, Daniel, s. John (1635), of Penpont, co. Brecon, minister. Jesus Coll., matric. 19 July, 1661, aged 18; father of the next, and of John 1684.
Richard, David, s. "Richard David," of Llanover, co. Cardigan, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 20; B.A. 1665, M.A. 1669 as Richards.
Price, Leoline, s. "Rice Davy," of Llywell, co. Brecon, pleb. Jesus Coll., matric. 22 March, 1661-2, aged 17; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1665.
Maurice, John, s. Ellis, of Llanvihangel, co. Carnarvon, pleb. p.p. Jesus Coll., matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 18; B.A. 1665, M.A. 1668.
Anwill, Evan, 'serv.,' s. William, of Dolgelly, co. Merioneth, p.p. Jesus Coll., matric. 17 May, 1661, aged 17.
